title: Docs version currency: 0.5.0 -> 1.0.0 across README/CLAUDE.md/docs
description:
## Objective
README and docs still state Apple Container 0.5.0+ — the shipped floor is 1.0.0. Update all NORMATIVE references; preserve historical/research notes as record.
## Verified scope (grep 2026-06-11)
- README.md: badge line 5 (0.5.0%2B), lines 37, 48, 93, 104, 405 — all normative, update to 1.0.0
- CLAUDE.md:52 Technology Requirements 'Apple Container 0.5.0+' -> 1.0.0+
- DO NOT touch kina-cli/tests/container_compat_tests.rs (frozen; its 0.5.0 mentions are negative guards asserting the stale text is gone)
- Re-grep for stale 0.9.0/0.12.x normative claims + install instructions (brew formula now ships 1.0.0 — verify README install section says brew install container or the pkg, accurately)
- docs/: research files describing old versions stay as history; only normative requirement statements update
## AC
- grep '0\.5\.0' README.md CLAUDE.md returns zero hits; docs/ hits are historical-only (each justified in the review)
- README install/requirements sections accurate for container 1.0.0
- mise run ci green
## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:documentation /core:container /core:git
close_reason: PR #18 squash-merged to main 00ebbc4; 0.5.0 refs gone from README/CLAUDE.md; main CI green post-merge

Branch docs/container-1.0.0-currency, 1 commit (2b0699e). Diff = 3 files: README.md, CLAUDE.md, .bees/issues.jsonl (tracker export only). No source, no tests, no scope creep.

(a) grep 0.5.0 README.md CLAUDE.md = ZERO hits (exit 1). PASS.
(b) Floor IS 1.0.0: kina-cli/src/core/apple_container.rs:16 'pub const MIN_VERSION: (u32,u32,u32) = (1,0,0)'; validate_version() rejects sub-1.0.0 with a 1.0.0 migration message. README/CLAUDE 1.0.0+ claims are factually correct. PASS.
(c) Remaining old-version hits repo-wide (tracked files only; .claude/worktrees + target/ are untracked scratch/build artifacts, ignored) each justified:
  - .bees/issues.jsonl: kina-27/kina-2/kina-1 titles+descriptions — tracker history of the migration itself. Justified.
  - docs/planning/beads-backlog-archive.json: by-name archive of superseded beads backlog; every 0.5.0/0.9.0 mention is a recorded historical/superseded item. Justified (history).
  - kina-cli/src/core/apple_container.rs (5x 'since 0.12.0'): factual cap-reduction comments explaining why --cap-add is needed; not normative floor claims. Justified (guards/explanatory).
  - kina-cli/tests/container_compat_tests.rs (0.12.3 / 0.5.0): frozen negative guards (T6/T7/T8) asserting sub-1.0.0 is rejected and the error must NOT contain stale 0.5.0 text. Justified (guards); explicitly out of scope.
(d) Install instructions accurate: independently verified on host — 'container --version' => 1.0.0; 'brew info container' => stable 1.0.0 (bottled). 'brew install container' (Option A) and signed .pkg (Option B) are both valid paths. PASS.
(e) No test files touched (git diff --name-only confirms). PASS.
(f) No scope creep — docs + tracker export only. PASS.

Minor non-blocking doc-quality nit: in the rewritten install section the 'Start Service'/'Verify' steps are numbered 3./4. after an intervening bold lead-in paragraph; Markdown will renumber them 1./2. Cosmetic only — content correct. Optional fixup.

Verdict: APPROVED for merge.

title: 0.1.0 release: semver-tag release action + mise github-backend installable binaries
description:
## Objective (operator 2026-06-11)
Tag kina 0.1.0 after docs are current. Needs: (a) release GitHub Action on semver tags, (b) asset naming that works with the mise github backend (ubi) so users can 'mise install' kina.
## Design
- Cargo.toml workspace version -> 0.1.0 (check current first)
- .github/workflows/release.yml: trigger on tags 'v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+' (MUST NOT collide with kernel-v* tags -> kernel-build.yml; verify both patterns are mutually exclusive)
- Build on macos runner (build needs no virtualization — only cluster tests do): cargo build --release, package tar.gz with ubi-compatible naming. Research /core:mise github backend + ubi asset-matching rules and pick naming that auto-resolves, e.g. kina-v0.1.0-aarch64-apple-darwin.tar.gz (binary 'kina' inside); include sha256 file. SHA-pin all actions; GITHUB_TOKEN env for mise
- README: install section gains mise instructions ('github:vinnie357/kina' tool spec) — lands AFTER kina-27 merges (avoid README conflict)
- LIVE VERIFICATION (Gate 2): after tagging v0.1.0 and the action publishes, actually run mise install with the github backend spec on this machine and 'kina --help' from the mise shim — paste verbatim
## AC
- tag v0.1.0 -> action green -> release with ubi-matchable assets + sha256
- mise github-backend install works on this host (verbatim evidence)
- kernel-build.yml untouched and non-overlapping
- mise run ci green
## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:tdd /core:git /core:mise /github:workflows /rust:rust /core:bees
close_reason: v0.1.0 released from main a6286ca; mise github-backend install live-verified

comments.text: OPERATOR CORRECTION (binding, 2026-06-11): use the mise GITHUB BACKEND, NOT ubi — ubi is deprecated. Tool spec target: 'github:vinnie357/kina' = '0.1.0' in mise.toml (same backend kina itself used for beads: github:steveyegge/beads@0.49.0). Implementer MUST: (1) read the current mise docs for the github backend's release-asset selection rules (os/arch token matching, any asset-pattern overrides available in tool options) — do not assume ubi conventions carry over; (2) name release assets so the github backend resolves them on macOS arm64 unambiguously (darwin/macos + arm64/aarch64 tokens; verify against the backend's actual matcher source/docs, cite it); (3) the live Gate-2 verification is 'mise install github:vinnie357/kina@0.1.0' (github backend syntax) on this host + kina --help via the mise shim, verbatim. Any ubi mention in the earlier description is superseded.

RUNNER RATIONALE (lead, answering operator question): macos runner is required for the RELEASE BUILD ONLY because the artifact must be aarch64-apple-darwin Mach-O — linux cross-compile needs the Apple SDK (license restricts to Apple hardware; osxcross/zig + native-tls framework linking is murky), and future codesign/notarization is macOS-only. Cost guard: release.yml fires on version tags only, mise+cargo caches, job = build/package/upload ONLY (no lint/test rerun — ubuntu ci.yml remains canonical). GITHUB_TOKEN env for mise per the ci.yml fix.

P1 PLAN (opus, design-only) — kina-28: 0.1.0 release action + mise github-backend installable binaries

== MISE GITHUB BACKEND ASSET RESOLUTION (research, verbatim-cited) ==
Source 1 — mise asset_matcher.rs (jdx/mise main) token regexes:
  macOS:  (?i)(?:\b|_)(?:darwin|mac(?:osx?)?|osx)(?:\b|_)     -> 'darwin' matches
  Arm64:  (?i)(?:\b|_)(?:aarch_?64|arm_?64)(?:\b|_)           -> BOTH 'aarch64' AND 'arm64' match
  github.rs delegates to asset_matcher::AssetMatcher::new().for_target(target).pick_from(assets);
  scoring = OS + arch + libc + archive-format + build-type (no debug). No asset_pattern needed when tokens are unambiguous.
Source 2 — real-world: github:steveyegge/beads (the backend kina itself uses) publishes
  beads_1.0.4_darwin_arm64.tar.gz (+ checksums.txt). Underscore-delimited <name>_<ver>_<os>_<arch>.tar.gz
  resolves on macOS arm64 with ZERO tool options.

DECISION — assetNaming: kina-v0.1.0-aarch64-apple-darwin.tar.gz (binary 'kina' at archive root) + a .sha256 sidecar.
  'aarch64' matches Arm64 regex; 'darwin' matches macOS regex; '.tar.gz' is a preferred generic archive.
  Tokens are surrounded by '-' which the regex accepts as a boundary ((?:\b|_) plus '-' word-break). The
  Rust target-triple form (aarch64-apple-darwin) is unambiguous and human-recognizable; 'apple' is inert noise
  to the matcher. NO mise tool-options required: 'mise install github:vinnie357/kina@0.1.0' auto-resolves.
  Fallback if the matcher ever scores ambiguously, document this opt for users (NOT required by our naming):
    "github:vinnie357/kina" = { version = "0.1.0", asset_pattern = "*aarch64-apple-darwin*" }
  Binary inside the tarball is named exactly 'kina' (from [[bin]] name='kina'), so default exe resolution works
  (no `exe`/`rename_exe` opt needed).

== TAG NON-COLLISION (AC) — proven both directions ==
release.yml trigger: tags: ['v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+']   (GHA glob: [0-9]=one digit, + = one-or-more of preceding,
  '.' literal). Matches v0.1.0 / v1.10.20. Requires tag to start 'v'+digit.
kernel-build.yml trigger: tags: ['kernel-v*']. Requires literal prefix 'kernel-'.
  -> 'kernel-v6.18.5-kina.1' starts with 'k' => CANNOT match v-digit pattern.
  -> 'v0.1.0' lacks 'kernel-' prefix => CANNOT match kernel-v*. Mutually exclusive. kernel-build.yml UNTOUCHED.

== CURRENT STATE (verified) ==
- Cargo.toml [workspace.package] version already = "0.1.0" (root Cargo.toml:8). kina-cli inherits version.workspace.
- CLI version surfaced via clap #[command(version)] (kina-cli/src/cli/mod.rs:25) => uses CARGO_PKG_VERSION => 0.1.0.
- Existing assert_cmd convention: Command::cargo_bin("kina").arg("--version") (cli_tests.rs:34-42).
- Existing source-grep guard convention: version_modernization_tests.rs reads files via env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")
  + std::fs::read_to_string + assert!(src.contains(...)). THIS legitimizes structural YAML-text assertions on release.yml.
- Pre-vetted SHA-pinned actions already in kernel-build.yml:
    actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683  # v4.2.2
    softprops/action-gh-release@c95fe1489396fe8a9eb87c0abf8aa5b2ef267fda  # v2.2.1
  setup-mise is a local composite (.github/actions/setup-mise) — re-use it (no jdx/mise-action needed; ci.yml uses composite).
- ci.yml sets `env: GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}` (the "GITHUB_TOKEN env for mise" fix referenced by operator).

== TEST LIST (P2 writes kina-cli/tests/release_meta_tests.rs — pub API + text-grep guards, all unit-level, no network) ==
T1 cargo_metadata_workspace_version_is_0_1_0:
    read root Cargo.toml via env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")/../Cargo.toml (manifest dir is kina-cli; parent is workspace root),
    parse with `toml` crate, assert workspace.package.version == "0.1.0". (toml is already a workspace dep.)
T2 cli_version_flag_reports_0_1_0:
    Command::cargo_bin("kina").arg("--version") -> success, stdout contains "0.1.0" (proves clap surfaces workspace ver).
T3 cli_version_matches_cargo_pkg_version:
    assert stdout of --version contains env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION") (consistency: build-time const == runtime CLI string).
T4 release_workflow_file_exists:
    Path manifest_dir/../.github/workflows/release.yml exists (read_to_string Ok). (Guard — P3 must deliver the file.)
T5 release_workflow_triggers_on_semver_tag_only:
    src contains "v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+" AND does NOT contain "branches:" trigger (build/package/upload only on tag).
T6 release_workflow_does_not_collide_with_kernel_tags:
    src does NOT contain "kernel-v"; AND kernel-build.yml still contains "kernel-v*" (untouched, mutual-exclusion guard).
T7 release_workflow_uses_macos_runner:
    src contains "runs-on: macos" (release build must be aarch64-apple-darwin Mach-O).
T8 release_workflow_actions_are_sha_pinned:
    every `uses:` line in release.yml matches @<40-hex> (regex over lines: no `uses:.*@v\d` / no bare `@main`).
T9 release_asset_naming_is_github_backend_resolvable:
    src contains the literal "aarch64-apple-darwin" AND "kina-v" AND ".sha256" (asset name + checksum sidecar present).
T10 release_workflow_sets_github_token_and_contents_write:
    src contains "GITHUB_TOKEN" AND "contents: write" (release upload perms + mise/gh auth).
  (T4-T10 mirror the existing version_modernization_tests.rs source-grep guard style — within repo convention.)
NON-TESTABLE (leave to P5 review / Gate-2 live verify, do NOT unit-test): actual `cargo build --release` output,
  real tar.gz byte contents, real `mise install github:vinnie357/kina@0.1.0` (network + published release required).

== P3 DELIVERABLES (implementer, not unit-tested) ==
1. .github/workflows/release.yml:
   on: push: tags: ['v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+']   (NO branch/PR triggers — cost guard; ubuntu ci.yml stays canonical)
   permissions: contents: write
   env: GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
   job runs-on: macos-latest (or macos-14 arm64); steps:
     - actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683  # v4.2.2
     - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-mise   (then mise trust --all && mise install)
     - cache: ~/.cargo + kina-cli/target keyed on Cargo.lock (mise+cargo cache per RUNNER RATIONALE)
     - cargo build --release (dir kina-cli)
     - package: VERSION="${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/}"; ASSET="kina-${VERSION}-aarch64-apple-darwin.tar.gz";
       tar -czf "$ASSET" -C kina-cli/target/release kina ; shasum -a 256 "$ASSET" > "$ASSET.sha256"
     - softprops/action-gh-release@c95fe1489396fe8a9eb87c0abf8aa5b2ef267fda  # v2.2.1
         tag_name: from ref; generate_release_notes: true; files: the .tar.gz + .sha256
   Comment block IN-FILE proving non-collision with kernel-v* (cite both patterns).
2. README install section (lands AFTER kina-27 to avoid conflict): mise github-backend snippet:
     mise.toml -> [tools] "github:vinnie357/kina" = "0.1.0"   (or `mise use github:vinnie357/kina@0.1.0`)
     prereq note: brew install + Apple Container runtime (macOS 26+) for runtime, not for install.

== EXECUTION ORDER (per CLAUDE.md gates) ==
P2 writes T1-T10 failing, runs full `mise run ci`, commits test:, pushes (frozen).
P3 implements release.yml + README without touching test files (git diff test-sha..HEAD on tests must be empty).
P4 runs `mise run ci` verbatim. P5 reviews AC coverage. Gate-2 live `mise install github:vinnie357/kina@0.1.0`
  happens ONLY after v0.1.0 is tagged & the action publishes (post-merge) — paste verbatim.

backendCitation: mise asset_matcher.rs Arm64 regex (?i)(?:\b|_)(?:aarch_?64|arm_?64)(?:\b|_) + macOS regex
  (?i)(?:\b|_)(?:darwin|mac(?:osx?)?|osx)(?:\b|_) => 'aarch64' and 'darwin' both score; confirmed by real
  github:steveyegge/beads asset beads_1.0.4_darwin_arm64.tar.gz resolving with no opts.
OPERATOR CONFIRMED runner split (2026-06-11): ubuntu for CI (ci.yml lint/test, unchanged), macos for build/release ONLY (release.yml, semver tags). No redirect — proceed as designed.

P4 CI Runner Report

## Status: PASS ✅

Branch: feat/release-0.1.0
Date: 2026-06-11

### Verbatim CI Output
```
mise WARN  env value contains '$' which will be expanded in a future release. Set `env_shell_expand = true` to opt in or `env_shell_expand = false` to keep current behavior and suppress this warning.
[ci] $ nu scripts/ci.nu
Running CI pipeline...
  fmt check...
  OK fmt check
  clippy...
  OK clippy
  test...
  OK test
CI passed
```

### Quality Gates
- Format check: PASS
- Clippy linter: PASS
- Test suite: PASS
- Overall: GREEN

Ready for merge.
MAIN-ONLY GATE added: ad906ae — release job fails fast unless the tagged commit is an ancestor of origin/main

Tag v0.1.0 at e0927df published NOTHING (release action run 27351247292 failed at Package binary step before release creation; zero consumers). Deleting and re-cutting the tag does not violate tag immutability, which protects published artifacts. Root cause: Cargo workspace places target/ at workspace root, not kina-cli/target/. Fixed in PR #20 (a6286ca).

RELEASED v0.1.0: https://github.com/vinnie357/kina/releases/tag/v0.1.0 | action run https://github.com/vinnie357/kina/actions/runs/27351811088 green | asset sha verified (1421f2e628f2f9135d2b223b505c251559c61e5da2e08440c4ff111e49bc7f97 matched .sha256) | mise install github:vinnie357/kina@0.1.0 resolved kina-v0.1.0-aarch64-apple-darwin.tar.gz | kina --version => kina 0.1.0

title: Remove beads entirely: kina tracks work in bees
description:
## Objective
Remove the legacy beads (bd) tracker from kina — bees (.bees/) is the tracker of record (operator, 2026-06-11: 'we can remove beads. we are using bees').

## Scope
- MIGRATE FIRST (do not lose the backlog): bd list --json > capture of all OPEN bd issues; port still-relevant open items into bees as backlog issues (type/priority preserved, description includes 'migrated from bd <id>'); items superseded by the kina-2 epic work get noted-and-skipped with reason. Archive the raw JSON export at docs/planning/beads-backlog-archive.json for history.
- rm -rf .beads/
- mise.toml: remove the beads tool pin (github:steveyegge/beads@0.49.0) and all bd:* tasks (bd:doctor, bd:list, bd:ready, bd:sync)
- AGENTS.md: replace the beads workflow section with the bees equivalent (bees ready / bees update -s in_progress / bees close; one issue = one branch = one PR unchanged)
- CLAUDE.md 'Task Tracking' section: beads -> bees (.bees/, prefix kina)
- README + scripts/ + docs/: grep -ri 'beads\|\bbd\b' and update/remove stale references (careful: 'bd' word-boundary, avoid false positives)
- CI side effect: steveyegge/beads no longer fetched by mise install (the rate-limit-prone dependency disappears)

## AC
- bd backlog migrated or consciously skipped (each open bd issue accounted for in the bees comment)
- zero beads/bd references left in mise.toml, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, README, scripts (grep evidence)
- mise install succeeds without the beads source; mise run ci green
- Fresh branch off post-#15 main: chore/remove-beads, single PR

## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:git /core:mise /core:bees /core:documentation
close_reason: PR #17 squash-merged to main (sha: 5a35571); beads removed, backlog migrated (10 -> kina-17..26) + archived; main CI green post-merge

title: Dead code removal: apple_provider.rs, provider.rs, utils, stub addons, kina-node image dir, unused manifests
description:
## Objective
Remove the dead code PR #14 identified (re-derived here; mechanical). ~2k lines.
## Scope (verify each is truly unreferenced before deleting; cite the grep)
- kina-cli/src/core/apple_provider.rs (828 lines, alternate provider unused by ClusterManager; contains --privileged calls that don't work on 1.0.0 anyway)
- kina-cli/src/core/provider.rs (365 lines, dead abstraction)
- kina-cli/src/utils/mod.rs (if unreferenced)
- Stub AddonType variants + match arms in cli/cluster.rs that return 'not yet implemented' (keep nginx-ingress + demo-app which ARE implemented)
- kina-cli/images/kina-node/ legacy dir (if present)
- kina-cli/manifests/kindnetd.yaml + bridge-cni.yaml (unreferenced by install code; also retires the kindnetd :stable floating tag from kina-13)
## AC
- cargo build + full mise run ci green after each removal batch
- zero grep hits for removed module names in remaining src
- kina --help unchanged except removed stub addons
- commit refs PR #14 as the identifying source
## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:git /core:mise /rust:rust /core:bees
close_reason: wf_b750a524-177: pure-deletion commit b1342c3 (-2003/+0), grep-evidenced unreferenced, CLI surface intact (install offers exactly nginx-ingress+demo-app), 300 tests green, opus approved

comments.text:
## Removal complete — grep evidence summary (commit b1342c3)

### core/apple_provider.rs (828 lines deleted)
- `grep -rn "apple_provider"` in src/tests/scripts/mise.toml: 1 hit only — `core/mod.rs:2:pub mod apple_provider;` (the declaration itself, now removed)
- Zero hits for `AppleProvider`, `apple_provider::`, or `use.*apple_provider` outside the deleted file

### core/provider.rs (365 lines deleted)
- `grep -rn "core::provider\|ContainerProvider\|use.*provider"` (excluding apple_provider + mod.rs): zero hits outside provider.rs itself
- `ClusterConfig` re-exported from config/mod.rs was consumed only by provider.rs — re-export also removed, resolving clippy unused-import error

### src/utils/mod.rs (230 lines deleted)
- `grep -rn "utils::\|use.*utils\|crate::utils"` outside utils dir: zero hits
- All submodules (fs, process, text, validate) marked `#[allow(dead_code)]`
- Removed from lib.rs pub mod list and main.rs mod declaration

### cli/cluster.rs — stub AddonType variants
- Removed: `IngressNginx`, `Cni`, `Coredns`, `MetricsServer` enum variants + their not-implemented match arms + methods returning `Err(anyhow!("... not yet implemented"))`
- Kept: `NginxIngress` (fully implemented with embedded manifests) and `DemoApp` (fully implemented)

### kina-cli/images/kina-node/ (directory deleted)
- `grep -rn "kina-node"` in src/tests/scripts/mise.toml: 2 hits — test fn name `d4_config_default_image_not_kina_node` (string literal in assert, not a path ref) and `scripts/image-test.nu:20` using `kina-node-test-` as a container name prefix (runtime string, not a dir reference). Neither references the image directory.

### kina-cli/manifests/kindnetd.yaml + bridge-cni.yaml (deleted)
- `grep -rn "kindnetd\|bridge-cni"` in src/tests/scripts/mise.toml: zero hits
- Neither manifest is referenced by any include_str! macro or install code path

### CI result: `mise run ci` — fmt OK, clippy OK, tests OK

CI runner: PASSED

mise run ci output (verbatim):
```
mise WARN  env value contains '$' which will be expanded in a future release. Set env_shell_expand = true to opt in or env_shell_expand = false to keep current behavior and suppress this warning.
[ci] $ nu scripts/ci.nu
Running CI pipeline...
  fmt check...
  OK fmt check
  clippy...
  OK clippy
  test...
  OK test
CI passed
```

All gates passed: format, clippy, tests. Ready for merge.

REVIEW VERDICT: APPROVED (reviewer, opus, read-only) — commit b1342c3

Re-ran all greps and behavioral checks against the working tree at b1342c3 (= HEAD).

title: macos-26 GH runner experiment: container 1.0.0 + minimal single-node cilium cluster in CI with cached kernel
description:
## Objective
Answer empirically: can a macos-26 GitHub runner run Apple Container 1.0.0 (Virtualization.framework inside the runner VM = nested virt question) and stand up a SUPER MINIMAL single-node kina cluster? If yes, ship the integration job.
## Design
- Stacked branch ci/macos26-integration off feat/container-1.0.0 (keeps PR #15 clean; folds in when green)
- New job/workflow: runs-on macos-26; install container 1.0.0 signed pkg (curl + sha256 verify + sudo installer); container system start --enable-kernel-install
- KERNEL CACHE (operator requirement): actions/cache keyed kernel-v6.18.5-kina.1; on miss download the Image asset from our GH release + sha256 verify f1a40c2c...; pass via --kernel-path
- Minimal cluster: kina create ci-test --cni cilium --kernel-path <cached> (single node, NO --workers); kina verify ci-test (single-node tolerated taint path); delete after
- Cost guard: workflow_dispatch + PR-label trigger only (not every push); vinnie flagged macos runner cost with rust builds — cache cargo target too or build on ubuntu and only run the cluster job on macos if artifact transfer is feasible
## AC
- Either: green run URL with cluster created + verify PASS in CI logs; or a precise findings report (exact failure: nested-virt denial, resource limits, time) posted to bees — both outcomes close the issue
- Bounded: max 5 CI iterations before reporting findings
## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:git /core:mise /core:container /github:workflows /core:nushell /core:bees
close_reason: wf_3287e788-6c4: INFEASIBLE on GH-hosted runners — verified across 3 deterministic attempts (run 27345209300): container system daemon starts fine, images pull, but first VZVirtualMachine boot fails with VZErrorDomain Code=2 'Virtualization is not available on this hardware' — the runner is itself a VM, nested Virtualization.framework denied. Only path to in-CI cluster tests: self-hosted bare-metal Apple silicon runner. ubuntu CI stays canonical; ci/macos26-integration branch kept unmerged as reference (incl. mise-based tool install 83a42d2). 10x macos billing multiplier noted even on the 3m16s probe.

comments.text: attempt 1: queued https://github.com/vinnie357/kina/actions/runs/27344675868
attempt 2: queued https://github.com/vinnie357/kina/actions/runs/27344963359
attempt 3: queued https://github.com/vinnie357/kina/actions/runs/27345209300

attempt 3: failed-at-step create-cluster https://github.com/vinnie357/kina/actions/runs/27345209300 FINAL FINDING: VZErrorDomain Code=2 Virtualization is not available on this hardware - nested virt is denied on macos-26 runners. container system start succeeds but container run fails when VZErrorDomain fires. Cannot proceed with kina cluster in CI on standard GH-hosted macos runners.

MACOS-26 FINDINGS: NOT GREEN — FUNDAMENTAL INFEASIBILITY (verified against run logs of https://github.com/vinnie357/kina/actions/runs/27345209300).

VERDICT: Standard GitHub-hosted macos-26 runners CANNOT run Apple Container VMs. Recommendation: close kina-15 as infeasible on GH-hosted runners; keep ubuntu CI; note for PR #14 discussion.

EVIDENCE (verbatim from job logs, job 80791941921, wall time 3m16s):
- 'Start container system' step PASSED: container system start --enable-kernel-install launched container-apiserver, installed the custom kernel, and 'Verifying machine API server is running' succeeded (exit 0). The userspace daemon is fine.
- 'Create single-node cluster (ci-test)' FAILED at step [6/6] Starting container, AFTER image fetch (327.3 MB), unpack (845.4 MB linux/arm64), kernel fetch, and init image unpack all succeeded. The cluster pipeline got all the way to VM boot.
- Decisive error verbatim:
  Error: failed to bootstrap container (cause: "internalError: "failed to bootstrap container ci-test-control-plane (cause: "unknown: "Error Domain=VZErrorDomain Code=2 "Virtualization is not available on this hardware." UserInfo={NSLocalizedFailure=Invalid virtual machine configuration., NSLocalizedFailureReason=Virtualization is not available on this hardware.}"")"")
- 'Verify cluster status' step was skipped (cluster never came up).

WHY FUNDAMENTAL (not operational): The failure is a Virtualization.framework hypervisor denial (VZErrorDomain Code=2) at the FIRST VZVirtualMachine boot, not a timeout/flake/resource exhaustion. The GH-hosted macos-26 runner is itself a VM; Apple's Virtualization.framework refuses to create a nested guest VM because nested virtualization is not exposed to the runner. container system start works (userspace), but every 'container run' that needs to boot a real Linux guest will hit this same wall. No cargo/kernel cache tuning, timeout bump, or workflow change can get past it — the hardware/entitlement boundary is the blocker.

REPRODUCIBILITY: 3/3 attempts (27344675868, 27344963359, 27345209300) reached this exact point. Deterministic, not flaky.

RECOMMENDATION:
1. Close kina-15 as infeasible-on-GH-hosted-runners.
2. Keep ubuntu CI as the canonical lane (unit/build tests already green there).
3. For PR #14: integration cluster testing on Apple Container requires a self-hosted bare-metal Apple-silicon runner (real hardware, no nesting) — that's the only path to in-CI cluster verification. Note runner cost: even this short 3m16s probe consumed billable macos-runner minutes (10x multiplier); a self-hosted Mac is the cost-effective + functional alternative.
4. Do NOT fold ci/macos26-integration into feat/container-1.0.0 — it cannot pass on GH-hosted runners.

MISE FIX: replaced curl kubectl (floating stable.txt) and dtolnay/rust-toolchain with SHA-pinned jdx/mise-action@dba19683ed58901619b14f395a24841710cb4925 (v4.1.0) + mise install per repo convention; also added kubectl=1.36.1 to mise.toml [tools] to pin the version. Commit 83a42d2. Iterator: pull before next fix.

CITATIONS for the infeasibility finding (source scout, 2026-06-11):
1. actions/runner-images#13505 — GitHub maintainer on record: 'The problem lies with the hardware and the hypervisor being used... the feature is still unavailable due to reasons beyond our control.' Host hypervisor does not expose EL2 to the macOS guest; kern.hv_support sysctl absent (also #9460: 'architectural issue. We can do nothing about it.')
2. apple/container#191 — maintainer (crosbymichael): 'nested virt is only supported for Linux guests' — i.e. even on M3+/macOS15 with EL2, a macOS-guest runner could NOT host container's Linux VMs; fkorotkov verified empirically on Tahoe-in-Tahoe.
3. apple/container docs/how-to.md: nested virt 'requires a M3 or newer Apple silicon machine' (bare-metal framing); error string 'nested virtualization is not supported on the platform'.
Net: GH-hosted macos runner = macOS guest VM -> (a) no EL2 from the outer hypervisor today AND (b) Virtualization.framework nesting is Linux-guest-only anyway. Two independent blockers; self-hosted bare-metal Apple silicon is the only CI path. Caveat: Apple developer-docs pages are JS-gated; the M3+/macOS15 EL2 specifics are corroborated by the three sources above rather than a verbatim Apple quote.

title: Version modernization: K8s 1.35.5 + kubeadm v1beta4, containerd 2.3.1 node image, pinned manifest images — REBUILD + REVALIDATE
description:
## Operator directive (2026-06-10)
'we will need to rebuild and revalidate these versions' — adopting the VERSION CURRENCY AUDIT (bees kina-2 comment) requires a node-image rebuild and a full goal revalidation, not just constant bumps.

## Scope (contract = the VERSION CURRENCY AUDIT matrix on kina-2)
- K8s v1.31.0 -> v1.35.5 (NOT 1.35.4 — kindest/node:v1.35.4 does not exist, verified 404; v1.35.5 verified) in cli default image, config defaults (incl. stale default_version v1.28.0 at config/mod.rs:184), kubeadm YAML kubernetesVersion
- kubeadm API v1beta3 -> v1beta4 across all 4 config templates (kubeletExtraArgs + extraArgs map->list). PREFER adopting via PR #14's hunks (correct per audit) with contributor authorship preserved — coordinate post-#15-merge rebase; re-derive only if the contributor is unresponsive
- Node image Dockerfile: KUBERNETES_VERSION arg-driven apt repo, containerd 2.3.1 (audit: 2.3.0 in PR is one patch stale), runc 1.4.2, CNI plugins 1.9.1, erofs-utils, debian:13-slim (stable as of 2026-06-10), kubelet bind-mount fix
- Manifests: nginx/nginx-ingress 5.1.1 -> 5.5.0; demo-app nginx:alpine (FLOATING, policy violation) -> nginx:1.30.2-alpine3.23
- mise.toml cilium-cli latest -> 0.19.4 pin; Cilium STAYS 1.18.10 (kina-3 decision)
- VERSION POLICY: every value pinned, every bump recorded

## REBUILD + REVALIDATE (binding, Gate 2)
- mise run image:build with new versions; image boots
- Full goal run on container 1.0.0 + custom kernel: kina create --workers 2 --cni cilium --kernel-path -> 3 Ready nodes on K8s v1.35.5, cilium-dbg status OK (kubeProxyReplacement true), kina verify PASS incl HTTP 200 :80 both workers — verbatim evidence in bees
- Acceptance bar from kina-6 applies verbatim: cilium status healthy AND nginx reached

## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:tdd /core:git /core:mise /core:container /rust:rust /rust:testing /core:nushell /core:bees

P1 TEST PLAN (opus test-planner) — kina-11 version modernization

## Test file
kina-cli/tests/version_modernization_tests.rs — pure unit/grep tests. NO container/kubectl spawns, NO network, NO fs writes, NO env mutation. async:N/A (sync fns). Mirrors verify_cmd_tests.rs adversarial-TDD convention: RED on compile/assert; P3 makes green WITHOUT editing this file.

## Import surface (BINDING for P3 implementer)
The kubeadm generators are PRIVATE today (generate_kubeadm_init_config @ apple_container.rs:1736; join worker config inline @ join_worker_node:1974). "pub API only" => P3 MUST extract pub free functions mirroring existing build_cilium_install_cmd / build_kubeadm_init_args pattern. Required pub fns in kina_cli::core::apple_container:

  pub fn generate_worker_join_config(worker_name, worker_ip, join_info:&KubeadmJoinInfo) -> String   (extract the inline format! from join_worker_node)
If P3 prefers to keep the method, also expose a thin pub wrapper. The "4 templates" = the 3 stanzas inside generate_kubeadm_init_config (InitConfiguration, ClusterConfiguration, JoinConfiguration) + the worker-join JoinConfiguration from generate_worker_join_config.
Manifest assertions read files via env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR") (repo convention, verify_cmd_tests T26/T27) — no new pub surface needed.

## Reference (credit)
kubeadm v1beta4 map->list migration mirrors external PR #14 hunks (gh pr diff 14, READ-ONLY). Commit messages cite PR #14.

---

## GROUP A — kubeadm v1beta4 apiVersion (positive + negative), all 4 templates

A1 init config emits apiVersion kubeadm.k8s.io/v1beta4 for InitConfiguration stanza
A2 init config emits apiVersion kubeadm.k8s.io/v1beta4 for ClusterConfiguration stanza
A3 init config emits apiVersion kubeadm.k8s.io/v1beta4 for JoinConfiguration stanza (control-plane init bundle)

## GROUP B — kubeletExtraArgs + extraArgs as name/value LISTS (the core map->list contract)

B1 init InitConfiguration kubeletExtraArgs uses list form: contains "- name: node-ip" AND "value: \"<vm_ip>\"" (assert literal substrings "- name: node-ip" and "- name: provider-id")
B2 init JoinConfiguration (control-plane bundle) kubeletExtraArgs list form: "- name: node-ip" / "- name: provider-id" present

(4) Running image refs PASS (kubectl -o jsonpath, verbatim) — nginx-ingress pods: nginx/nginx-ingress:5.5.0 (both); demo pods: nginx:1.30.2-alpine3.23 (4 replicas).
(5) Own curls PASS — Host: kina-test-control-plane.flame.local to http://192.168.65.48:80 and http://192.168.65.49:80 -> HTTP 200, body marker <title>Kina Demo Success!</title>.
(6) kina verify PASS — target/release/kina verify kina-test EXIT=0; Apple Container 1.0.0 meets minimum; Nodes 3/3, Cilium DS 3/3, HTTP probes 200 + demo marker both workers.

(8) Host default kernel symlink UNTOUCHED PASS — default.kernel-arm64 -> vmlinux-6.12.28-153 (Jun 28 2025); system config default kernel is kata vmlinux-6.18.15-186; kina kernel applied per-container via kernel.bin, host default not mutated.
(9) Embedded manifests floating-tag check PASS — the include_str! manifests (6 nginx-ingress files + demo-app.yaml) have NO floating tags: daemonset pins nginx/nginx-ingress:5.5.0, demo-app pins nginx:1.30.2-alpine3.23. NOTE (non-blocking, NOT embedded): manifests/kindnetd.yaml uses ghcr.io/aojea/kindnetd:stable (alternative CNI, not used in cilium goal run), examples/ingress/*.yaml use nginx:alpine (user docs), config_tests.rs has a fixture string 'image:latest' — none are compiled into the goal run.

All 9 checks verified live. Cluster left RUNNING, nothing modified.
KERNEL RELEASE GRADUATED: https://github.com/vinnie357/kina/releases/tag/kernel-v6.18.5-kina.1

title: Harden kina verify: empty node-IP list must FAIL; remove stale README Quick Start Summary
description:
## From kina-9 P5 review (non-blocking follow-ups, operator bar 'never green on no evidence')
1. cli/cluster.rs:926 guards HTTP aggregation with 'if !node_ips.is_empty()'; get_cluster_status errors are swallowed to vec![] (L874-883) so the HTTP probe layer silently skips and verify can PASS with zero HTTP evidence — contradicts the aggregate_empty_is_false principle already in the unit tests. Fix: empty node_ips (or status fetch error) => all_pass=false with an explicit FAIL line.
2. README.md L9-15 'Quick Start Summary' still shows the old kina export + KUBECONFIG flow, competing with the canonical ## Quick Start (L116-123). Remove/align.
## AC
- Failing test first (verify_cmd_tests or new file — verify_cmd_tests.rs is FROZEN from kina-9; put new tests in kina-cli/tests/verify_hardening_tests.rs)
- kina verify exits non-zero when node IPs cannot be determined
- One canonical Quick Start in README
- mise run ci green
## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:tdd /core:git /rust:rust /rust:testing
close_reason: single-agent TDD: test 967d40d + fix 83aab8b, http_layer_pass() never green on empty node_ips, stale README summary removed, CI green (36 tests)

comments.text: RED sha: 967d40d test(verify): verify_hardening_tests.rs (6 tests, compile-error RED on missing http_layer_pass). GREEN sha: 83aab8b fix(verify): empty node-IP list sets all_pass=false with explicit FAIL line; remove stale README Quick Start Summary. CI evidence (mise run ci): fmt check OK, clippy OK, test OK (36 tests: 30 verify_cmd_tests + 6 verify_hardening_tests), CI passed. Changes: verify.rs added http_layer_pass() returning false for empty node_ips; cluster.rs removed if !node_ips.is_empty() bypass guard, added FAIL println when empty, calls http_layer_pass+aggregate_verify unconditionally; README.md removed stale Quick Start Summary L9-15.

title: First-class verification UX: kina install demo-app, kina verify, embedded manifests, cilium-aware status
description:
## Objective
The operator must be able to validate a cluster with kina commands alone — no sed, no container exec, no KUBECONFIG exports, no repo-root CWD requirement. Source: operator 2026-06-10 'we need to fix this, I should be able to run those'.

## Acceptance criteria
1. 'kina install demo-app --cluster <name>': applies the demo-app manifest EMBEDDED in the binary (include_str!), substituting CLUSTER_NAME and DNS_DOMAIN internally (domain auto-detected from 'container system dns list', fallback 'test'); waits for app pods Ready; works from ANY directory.
2. nginx-ingress manifests embedded likewise — fixes cli/cluster.rs:496-499 resolving manifests relative to CWD ('kina install nginx-ingress' currently only works from repo root).
3. NEW 'kina verify [cluster]': one command that checks and prints PASS/FAIL lines with evidence: (a) expected node count all Ready, (b) Cilium DaemonSet ready n/n + operator healthy (reuse/extend the existing CNI panel logic at cli/cluster.rs:1115-1156), (c) HTTP probe: curl each worker node IP on :80 with the derived Host header, expect 200 + demo marker. Non-zero exit on any FAIL. Pure helper fns unit-tested (probe URL/host construction, pass/fail aggregation); no live cluster in unit tests.
4. 'kina status' cilium panel verified working against container 1.0.0 output (it shells kubectl; ensure kubeconfig/context resolution works when invoked from anywhere).
5. scripts/test-cluster-multi.nu + mise test:cluster:multi accept CNI and KERNEL_PATH (env KINA_TEST_CNI / KINA_TEST_KERNEL_PATH) and pass them to kina create.
6. README quickstart updated to exactly: kina create kina-test --workers 2 --cni cilium --kernel-path <path>; kina install nginx-ingress --cluster kina-test; kina install demo-app --cluster kina-test; kina verify kina-test. (kernel-path goes away when kina-8 lands auto-download.)
7. mise run ci green.

## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:tdd /core:git /core:mise /core:container /rust:rust /rust:testing /core:nushell /core:bees
close_reason: five-tier wf_08a6a436-d24: test 70ed2ed, impl 8eb653b, CI green 0 fix cycles, P5 approved; kina install demo-app + kina verify + embedded manifests on PR #15; 2 non-blocking follow-ups -> kina-10

comments.text:
P1 TEST PLAN (kina-9 — first-class verify UX) — tests in kina-cli/tests/verify_cmd_tests.rs, pub-API only, pure (no live container/kubectl CLI, no spawns, no network). Mirrors the export+source-grep pattern of cilium_install_tests.rs / kernel_ebpf_tests.rs. Sequenced simplest-first. RED-by-compile is the spec; P3 implements without editing this file. Lands on open PR #15 branch.

## DESIGN DECISIONS (binding for P2/P3)

New pure-fn home: module `kina_cli::core::verify` (re-export `pub mod verify;` in core/mod.rs). All pure helpers below are `pub fn` in that module. CLI wiring (install demo-app variant, `verify` subcommand) stays in cli/cluster.rs. Embedded manifests via `include_str!` in cli/cluster.rs replacing the CWD-relative path at L496-499 (nginx-ingress) and the demo-app file.

- `pub fn render_demo_manifest(manifest: &str, cluster: &str, domain: &str) -> String` — substitutes BOTH `${CLUSTER_NAME}` → cluster and `${DNS_DOMAIN}` → domain in the input manifest string. Pure string transform; the embedded demo-app.yaml is the runtime input (via include_str!), but the fn takes the manifest str as a param so it is unit-testable with a small fixture. MUST NOT shell out to envsubst (only `$MY_POD_*` runtime vars are left for the pod's own envsubst; CLUSTER_NAME/DNS_DOMAIN are substituted at render time by this fn). Verified placeholders in manifests/demo-app.yaml: `${CLUSTER_NAME}` (L178), `${CLUSTER_NAME}-control-plane.${DNS_DOMAIN}` (L214, L280).

- `pub fn probe_host(cluster: &str, domain: &str) -> String` — returns the Host-header value `"<cluster>-control-plane.<domain>"`. Mirrors test-cluster-validate.nu L51 `$"($latest_demo)-control-plane.($dns_domain)"`.

- `pub fn probe_url(node_ip: &str) -> String` — returns `"http://<node_ip>"` (the curl target; Host header carried separately). Mirrors validate.nu L88 `$"http://($ip)"`. (Split host from URL so the verify command builds `curl -H "Host: <probe_host>" <probe_url>` — the two-arg shape the existing nu script uses.)

- `pub fn parse_dns_domain(dns_list_output: &str) -> String` — parses the first non-empty trimmed line of `container system dns list` stdout; returns `"test"` when output is empty/all-blank (AC1/AC3 fallback). Mirrors validate.nu L41-46 first-non-empty-line logic BUT replaces its hard `err` on empty with the literal fallback `"test"` (AC mandates fallback "test", not error).

- Verify result model: `pub struct ProbeResult { pub node: String, pub passed: bool }` (or equivalent with a node label + bool). `pub fn aggregate_verify(results: &[ProbeResult]) -> bool` returns true IFF the slice is non-empty AND every result passed (matches validate.nu semantics: zero probes = fail, any fail = fail). P3 may name the struct/fields differently but the asserted pub symbols below must exist.

- `pub fn probe_passed(body: &str) -> bool` — true IFF the HTTP response body contains the demo marker `"Kina Demo Success"`. Verified marker source: manifests/demo-app.yaml L64 `<title>Kina Demo Success!</title>` (survives render — not a substituted placeholder); validate.nu L90/L129 use `str contains "Kina Demo Success"`.

- CLI: `AddonType` (cli/cluster.rs L185-200, clap::ValueEnum) gains a `#[value(name = "demo-app")] DemoApp` variant; the `execute` match (L465-481) gains an `AddonType::DemoApp => self.install_demo_app(...)` arm. A NEW top-level `verify` subcommand (clap) takes an optional `[cluster]` positional and exits non-zero on FAIL aggregation. Both asserted via source-grep guards only (CLI wiring + live behavior are review-gated, not unit-tested).

- Embedded manifests: cli/cluster.rs uses `include_str!("../../manifests/demo-app.yaml")` and `include_str!` for each nginx-ingress manifest, removing the `std::env::current_dir()` + `.join("kina-cli").join("manifests")` CWD resolution at L508-520. Asserted via source-grep guards (no `current_dir()`-derived manifest path remains).

## TEST LIST (name :: criterion)

# Group A — render_demo_manifest (demo-app substitution) — simplest
1. render_substitutes_cluster_name :: render_demo_manifest("host: ${CLUSTER_NAME}-control-plane.${DNS_DOMAIN}", "kina-test", "test") contains "kina-test-control-plane.test" and contains NO literal "${CLUSTER_NAME}".
2. render_substitutes_dns_domain :: render output for cluster="c", domain="mydomain" contains "mydomain" and contains NO literal "${DNS_DOMAIN}".
3. render_triangulation_distinct_values :: render_demo_manifest(fixture, "alpha", "beta") contains "alpha-control-plane.beta" and does NOT contain "kina-test" (values interpolated, not hardcoded).
4. render_leaves_pod_runtime_vars_untouched :: render_demo_manifest("x ${MY_POD_NAME} ${CLUSTER_NAME}", "c", "d") still contains literal "${MY_POD_NAME}" (only CLUSTER_NAME/DNS_DOMAIN are render-time; MY_POD_* stay for the pod's own envsubst) AND contains "c".
5. render_substitutes_all_occurrences :: an input with two "${CLUSTER_NAME}" occurrences yields zero remaining "${CLUSTER_NAME}" after render (global, not first-only).

# Group B — probe_host construction
6. probe_host_basic :: probe_host("kina-test", "test") == "kina-test-control-plane.test".
7. probe_host_triangulation :: probe_host("prod", "local") == "prod-control-plane.local" (and != the Group-6 value — domain+cluster both templated).

# Group C — probe_url construction
8. probe_url_prefixes_http :: probe_url("10.0.0.5") == "http://10.0.0.5".
9. probe_url_triangulation :: probe_url("192.168.65.3") == "http://192.168.65.3" (IP interpolated, not hardcoded).

# Group D — parse_dns_domain (with fallback "test")
10. parse_dns_domain_single_line :: parse_dns_domain("test\n") == "test".
11. parse_dns_domain_distinct_value :: parse_dns_domain("mydomain\n") == "mydomain" (triangulation — not hardcoded "test").
12. parse_dns_domain_trims_whitespace :: parse_dns_domain("  example  \n") == "example" (leading/trailing trimmed, mirrors validate.nu str trim).
13. parse_dns_domain_empty_falls_back_to_test :: parse_dns_domain("") == "test" (AC1/AC3 fallback).
14. parse_dns_domain_blank_lines_fall_back_to_test :: parse_dns_domain("\n  \n") == "test" (all-blank output → fallback, not empty string).
15. parse_dns_domain_first_nonblank_line :: parse_dns_domain("\nactualdomain\nignored\n") == "actualdomain" (skips leading blank, takes first non-empty — mirrors validate.nu first-non-empty logic).

# Group E — probe_passed (demo marker detection)
16. probe_passed_true_on_marker :: probe_passed("...<title>Kina Demo Success!</title>...") == true.
17. probe_passed_false_without_marker :: probe_passed("<html>404 not found</html>") == false.
18. probe_passed_false_on_empty_body :: probe_passed("") == false (empty/no-response body is a FAIL, not a pass).

# Group F — aggregate_verify (pass/fail aggregation from probe results)
19. aggregate_all_pass_is_true :: aggregate_verify(&[ProbeResult{passed:true,...}, {passed:true,...}]) == true (every probe passed).
20. aggregate_any_fail_is_false :: aggregate_verify(&[{passed:true}, {passed:false}]) == false (one FAIL fails the whole verify — non-zero exit).
21. aggregate_empty_is_false :: aggregate_verify(&[]) == false (zero probes = FAIL; mirrors validate.nu "All ingress tests failed" when passed==0 — never green on no evidence).
22. aggregate_single_pass_is_true :: aggregate_verify(&[{passed:true}]) == true (boundary: one passing node is a PASS).
23. aggregate_all_fail_is_false :: aggregate_verify(&[{passed:false},{passed:false}]) == false.

# Group G — CLI wiring source-grep guards (cli/cluster.rs) — live behavior is review-gated, NOT unit-tested
24. source_addontype_has_demo_app_variant :: cli/cluster.rs contains a clap value name "demo-app" (AddonType gains the DemoApp variant — AC1 `kina install demo-app`).
25. source_has_verify_subcommand :: cli/cluster.rs (or the command-enum source) contains a "verify" subcommand wiring (AC3 `kina verify [cluster]`).
26. source_demo_manifest_embedded_via_include_str :: cli/cluster.rs contains `include_str!` referencing "demo-app.yaml" (AC1 embedded manifest).
27. source_nginx_manifests_embedded_via_include_str :: cli/cluster.rs contains `include_str!` referencing an nginx-ingress manifest path (AC2 — nginx manifests embedded).
28. source_no_cwd_relative_manifest_resolution :: cli/cluster.rs does NOT contain the CWD-relative manifest join `.join("kina-cli")` (the L508-520 current_dir()-derived manifest path is removed; manifests resolve from the binary, not CWD — AC2 "works from any directory"). Guards against the embed being added while the old CWD path lingers.
29. source_install_calls_render_demo_manifest :: cli/cluster.rs references render_demo_manifest (the install demo-app path renders the embedded manifest through the pure fn — proves the fn is wired, not dead code; AC1 substitution happens internally).
30. source_verify_uses_aggregate :: cli/cluster.rs references aggregate_verify (the verify command's PASS/FAIL/exit-code decision routes through the pure aggregation fn — AC3 non-zero exit on any FAIL).

## OUT OF SCOPE (review-gated, NOT in this test file)
- AC4 (kina status cilium panel against container 1.0.0 live output) — shells kubectl; live behavior, no pure surface to unit-test. Left to review + manual verify on a live cluster.
- AC5 (scripts/test-cluster-multi.nu + mise test:cluster:multi accept KINA_TEST_CNI / KINA_TEST_KERNEL_PATH and pass to kina create) — nu-script + mise.toml change. The task instruction: assert by grepping rendered script/task content ONLY if testable from a Rust integration test; nu/toml content is not naturally reachable from cargo test. Recommend leaving AC5 to review (verify the env vars are read in test-cluster-multi.nu and the create invocation passes `--cni`/`--kernel-path`). If P3 wants a guard, a source-grep test reading scripts/test-cluster-multi.nu via CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR + "../scripts/test-cluster-multi.nu" asserting it contains "KINA_TEST_CNI" and "KINA_TEST_KERNEL_PATH" is OPTIONAL and acceptable, but the live passthrough (env → create flag) is review-gated.
- AC6 (README quickstart text) — doc change, content-grep is tautological; review-gated.
- AC7 (mise run ci green) — P4 CI runner gate, not a test in this file.
- Live cluster behavior for AC1/AC2/AC3 (pod-Ready waits, kubectl apply, real HTTP curl, real node-count/Cilium-DaemonSet checks) — explicitly NOT unit-tested per the contract; only the pure helpers are.

## NOTES FOR P2 (test author)
- Import surface: `use kina_cli::core::verify::{render_demo_manifest, probe_host, probe_url, parse_dns_domain, probe_passed, aggregate_verify, ProbeResult};`. Compile errors on the not-yet-existing module/symbols ARE the red spec.
- Source-grep guards (Group G) read src via `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR + "src/cli/cluster.rs"`, pattern-matching the read result with `match std::fs::read_to_string(&p) { Ok(s) => s, Err(e) => panic!("...: {}", e) }` — NO bang fns anywhere (project rule: no File.read_to_string().unwrap(), no `.unwrap()`/`.expect()` in tests; use match/panic per cilium_install_tests.rs L45-48).
- ProbeResult literal construction in tests: if P3 names fields differently than {node, passed}, P2 should still assert through the two pub symbols the plan freezes (ProbeResult constructor + aggregate_verify). Keep the struct minimal — a bool-bearing result per node.
- No `async`, no `#[tokio::test]` — all helpers are sync pure fns. No tempfiles, no FS writes, no env mutation (pure string fns only) → all tests trivially isolated, async-safe.
- For Group A use a SMALL inline fixture string with the two placeholders, NOT the full embedded demo-app.yaml (keep the test focused on substitution semantics, not the 290-line manifest).
- T28 negative guard: assert the exact current substring `.join("kina-cli")` is ABSENT (verified present today at cli/cluster.rs L511). If P3's embed refactor leaves any other CWD-relative manifest resolution, add the matching negative — but `.join("kina-cli")` is the load-bearing one.

## ANTI-FABRICATION / VERIFICATION BASIS
Verified by reading: manifests/demo-app.yaml (placeholders `${CLUSTER_NAME}` @L178, `${CLUSTER_NAME}-control-plane.${DNS_DOMAIN}` @L214+L280; marker `Kina Demo Success` @L64; pod runtime vars MY_POD_NAME/MY_POD_IP/MY_NODE_NAME @L25-36 left for in-pod envsubst). cli/cluster.rs: install_nginx_ingress L490-520 (CWD-relative manifest resolution via current_dir().join("kina-cli").join("manifests"), the AC2 bug); AddonType enum L185-200 (clap::ValueEnum, 5 variants, no demo-app yet); execute match L465-481; CNI ready panel check_cni_ready L1128-1179 (AC3(b) reuse target). scripts/test-cluster-validate.nu: Host header L51, http://ip probe L88, marker check L90/L129, DNS first-non-empty-line parse + hard-err-on-empty L40-49 (AC3 replaces err with fallback "test"). scripts/test-cluster-multi.nu: no KINA_TEST_CNI/KERNEL plumbing today (AC5 adds it). mise.toml test:cluster:multi L118-121. tests/cilium_install_tests.rs L1-54 (export+grep pattern, match/panic on file read, no bang fns, pure). kina-cli/src/lib.rs (pub mod core). STAGE: design only, no code written.

CI passed

```
mise WARN  env value contains '$' which will be expanded in a future release. Set `env_shell_expand = true` to opt in or `env_shell_expand = false` to keep current behavior and suppress this warning.
[ci] $ nu scripts/ci.nu
Running CI pipeline...
  fmt check...
  OK fmt check
  clippy...
  OK clippy
  test...
  OK test
CI passed
```

Status: GREEN

P5 REVIEWER VERDICT (opus) — APPROVED. Verified all 7 ACs against commits 70ed2ed (tests) + 8eb653b (impl) on feat/container-1.0.0.

EVIDENCE:
- AC1 (install demo-app, embedded, internal substitution, DNS auto-detect+fallback, pod-Ready wait, any-dir): cli/cluster.rs:639-741 — include_str!(demo-app.yaml) L671, render_demo_manifest L672, parse_dns_domain w/ test fallback L657-668, kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready L711-723. PASS.
- AC2 (nginx embedded, no CWD path): grep current_dir = ZERO; grep .join(kina-cli) = ZERO; all 6 nginx manifests via include_str! L526-554. T28 negative guard green. PASS.
- AC3 (kina verify, PASS/FAIL+evidence, non-zero exit, pure fns unit-tested): VerifyArgs::execute L744-947 — node-count L776-811, Cilium DS L813-856, HTTP probe L858-924, std::process::exit(1) on FAIL aggregation L944. 30/30 pure-helper tests pass. PASS.
- AC5 (multi.nu + mise accept KINA_TEST_CNI/KINA_TEST_KERNEL_PATH): test-cluster-multi.nu L17-19 env read, L46-51 passthrough to --cni + conditional --kernel-path; mise.toml task doc updated. PASS.
- AC6 (README 4-cmd quickstart): Quick Start L116-123 is EXACTLY create --workers 2 --cni cilium --kernel-path / install nginx-ingress / install demo-app / verify. PASS.
- AC7 (CI green): 30/30 verify_cmd_tests pass locally; bees comment shows ci.nu GREEN. PASS.
- Frozen files: prior-phase test files (kernel_ebpf, cilium_install, container compat) UNTOUCHED. P3 did NOT modify P2 test file (git diff 70ed2ed..8eb653b on verify_cmd_tests.rs = empty). PASS.

UX BAR (create/install/install/verify from any dir, no other tooling): MET. core/cluster.rs:278-281 — create operates on ~/.kube/<cluster> (Apple Container writes kubeconfig there during create); install + verify read the same path (cli/cluster.rs:644, 769). No kina export / KUBECONFIG / sed / container exec. Manifests embedded => any directory. 4-command flow is self-contained.

NON-BLOCKING FOLLOW-UPS (do not gate merge):
1. verify HTTP-probe gap: L926 guards aggregation with if !node_ips.is_empty(); if get_cluster_status fails it swallows to vec![] (L874-883) and the HTTP layer is silently skipped without setting all_pass=false. Cluster could report PASS on nodes+cilium alone with zero HTTP evidence — weakens AC3 200+marker guarantee and contradicts the aggregate_empty_is_false never-green-on-no-evidence principle. Recommend: treat empty node_ips as FAIL.
2. Stale README top summary L9-15 (Quick Start Summary) still shows OLD kina export + KUBECONFIG flow, competing with AC6-compliant Quick Start at L116. Cosmetic doc cleanup.

title: Phase A spike: build custom kernel (6.18.5+kina.1) and validate full-eBPF Cilium on a 3-node cluster
description:
## Objective
Execute the phase-A manual spike from the kina-5 KERNEL PROPOSAL: build the pinned custom kernel via apple/containerization kernel/ tooling (tag 0.33.3, linux 6.18.5) with the Cilium config fragment, boot-smoke it via per-container --kernel (ZERO system mutation: no kernel set, no config.toml, no service restart), then stand up a 3-node cluster with FULL-eBPF Cilium: kubeProxyReplacement=true, l7Proxy=true, transparent DNS proxy on, bpf.masquerade=true, bpf.hostLegacyRouting=false, hubble enabled.

## Contract
The binding spec is the KERNEL PROPOSAL comment on kina-5 (config fragment, exact commands, verification checklist = this issue's AC). Resolve its three open hypotheses: (1) BPF_JIT resolved value on the built kernel, (2) per-container --kernel precedence, (3) build wall-time + Image size.

## Key facts (verified by kina-5 research)
- Stock kata vmlinux has CONFIG_MODULES off — missing options are unfixable at runtime; rebuild is the only path
- Apple containerization config-arm64 already fixes both kina-4 blockers (xt_socket, multiple routing tables); fragment adds BPF_JIT(+ALWAYS_ON), BPF_EVENTS(+FTRACE/KPROBES), BPF_STREAM_PARSER, CRYPTO_USER_API_HASH, SCHEDSTATS, INET_DIAG_DESTROY, optional NETKIT/DEBUG_INFO_BTF
- This host's ACTIVE kernel is stale vmlinux-6.12.28-153 (June 2025) — container upgrades never bump an installed kernel; 1.0.0 recommends kata 3.28.0 vmlinux-6.18.15-186
- container run -k/--kernel <path> boots ONE container on a custom kernel — kina node containers only, other workloads untouched

## Acceptance criteria
Per the kina-5 proposal section-4 verification checklist, plus: all versions pinned (KSOURCE URL, base-config tag, cilium 1.18.x, sha256 of built Image) per the VERSION POLICY; evidence verbatim in bees comments; retired workarounds confirmed absent from the new install command.

## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:container /core:tdd /core:mise /core:bees
close_reason: wf_d5de8158-6e8 done: kernel 6.18.5-kina.1 built (32MB, sha f1a40c2c), test 4c06271 + impl 4e3e183 + fix 98d26c7 (4g/4cpu nodes + CSR approval pre-gate), CI green; opus live-verified: 3 Ready nodes on custom kernel, kube-proxy-replacement=true, no kube-proxy DS, zero workaround keys, cilium-dbg status OK 26/26, HTTP 200 nginx :80 both workers, host default kernel untouched

## DESIGN DECISIONS (binding for P2/P3)
- New pub fn `build_cilium_install_cmd_ebpf(version, cp_ip) -> String` is the FULL-eBPF profile builder (separate from the existing stock `build_cilium_install_cmd`). This keeps the 22 frozen cilium_install_tests.rs assertions green (they pin the stock workaround profile) while adding the new profile as its own testable surface. P3 may instead refactor to a profile-enum param, but the NEW pub symbol asserted here must exist and produce the full-eBPF string.
- New pub fn `build_kubeadm_init_args(full_ebpf: bool) -> Vec<String>` extracted from the inline args in run_kubeadm_init (apple_container.rs ~L1698-1707); returns the kubeadm-init arg vector. When full_ebpf=true it includes "--skip-phases=addon/kube-proxy" (kubeProxyReplacement=true requires kube-proxy absent); when false it omits it. This is the pure, assertable surface for the kubeadm/kube-proxy-skip requirement.
- Config: ClusterDefaults gains `node_kernel_path: Option<PathBuf>` (config-file optional setting); CreateClusterOptions gains `node_kernel_path: Option<PathBuf>` (CLI override). CreateArgs gains `--kernel-path <PATH>` (clap, optional). New pub fn `select_kernel_path(cli_flag: Option<PathBuf>, config_default: Option<PathBuf>) -> Option<PathBuf>` mirrors select_cni precedence.
- New pub fn `node_kernel_args(kernel_path: Option<&Path>) -> Vec<String>` returns ["--kernel", "<abs-path>"] when Some, [] when None. Used by all three node-creation fns (create_single_node, create_control_plane_node, create_worker_node). Source-grep guards confirm each of the three call sites references it.
- The full-eBPF profile is selected when node_kernel_path is Some (custom kernel). Stock (None) keeps the existing workaround profile MINUS the bpf.xtSocketFallback line (the wrong-direction a81a825 mitigation).

## Candidate 2: cilium/little-vm-helper
- Checked via curl API: releases v0.0.26–v0.0.30 — ALL have NO_ASSETS in GitHub releases.
- LVH ships kernels via quay.io/lvh-images/kernel-images (confirmed: 6.18, 6.12, 6.6, 5.10, bpf, bpf-next tags exist). Images are OCI multi-arch but the kernel is embedded inside a qcow2 disk image, NOT as a standalone ARM64 Image file. Per LVH README: 'Only amd64 images contain a compatible bootloader. So even though kernels are present in the arm64 images, you'll need to supply it to QEMU through the --kernel lvh option flag.' Even if extracted, these kernels target QEMU; format compatibility with Apple Virtualization.framework is unverified and no arm64 standalone Image binary is available.
- RESULT: Wrong format for Apple Container; no standalone arm64 Image binary published. ELIMINATED.

Cilium agent status (crictl exec, captured at Restart 2 stable window, 01:39:36Z):
  KubeProxyReplacement: True [eth0 192.168.65.21] — CONFIRMED
  Routing: Network: Tunnel [vxlan]   Host: BPF — BPF HOST ROUTING CONFIRMED
  Masquerading: BPF [eth0] 10.244.0.0/24 — BPF MASQUERADE CONFIRMED
  Hubble: Ok Current/Max Flows: 23/4095 — HUBBLE ENABLED CONFIRMED
  Proxy Status: OK, ip 10.244.0.133, 0 redirects active on ports 10000-20000, Envoy: external — L7 PROXY ENABLED
  Controller Status: 17/17 healthy

NOTE: Cilium agent subsequently entered restart loop (6+ restarts) due to cilium-operator leader election cascade; cluster is in degraded state at time of report.

## STEP 5/6 — nginx / HTTP probe: SKIPPED (workers not created, cluster not stable)

## VERDICT: PARTIAL
- PASS: Custom kernel 6.18.5-kina.1 boots on control-plane (zero system mutation)
- PASS: All 18 required eBPF kernel config options =y
- PASS: Full-eBPF Cilium config deployed (kubeProxyReplacement=true, l7Proxy=true, bpf.masquerade=true, hostLegacyRouting=false, hubble=true, transparent DNS on)
- PASS: kube-proxy absent
- PASS: KubeProxyReplacement: True confirmed from cilium-agent status
- PASS: BPF host routing and BPF masquerade confirmed
- FAIL: Workers not created (3-node cluster not achieved)
- FAIL: nginx and HTTP probe not completed
- ROOT CAUSE: cilium-operator leader election cascade during early startup causes readiness gate timeout, which aborts worker creation. Need either longer --wait timeout or a fix to the cilium-operator/apiserver bootstrapping sequence.

FIX CYCLE 1 — root cause + fix (sha 98d26c7)

## Root cause (two compounding failures)

### 1. Resource starvation (OOM → crash cascade → TLS errors)
Node containers launched at Apple Container default: 4 vCPUs / 1024 MB. Full-eBPF Cilium runs etcd + apiserver + KCM + scheduler + cilium-agent + cilium-operator + Envoy DaemonSet + Hubble relay in the single control-plane VM. OOM kills cascade into control-plane component restarts that surface as TLS internal errors — which look identical to the CSR/leader-election failure described below.

FIX: pass --cpus 4 --memory 4g on all three node-creation paths (create_single_node, create_control_plane_node, create_worker_node) in apple_container.rs.

### 2. CSR TLS cascade → cilium-operator leader election loop
kubeadm sets serverTLSBootstrap: true in the kubelet config. This means kubelet generates a serving cert via CertificateSigningRequest (not self-signed). The CSR stays Pending until explicitly approved. Any path through the kubelet serving port (:10250) — including cilium status --wait probes that exec into pods via the kubelet API — fails with 'remote error: tls: internal error' until approved. cilium-operator loses leader election on every such failure, then hits a 5-minute backoff before retrying. Six or more crashes exhaust the 5-minute readiness gate.

FIX: inside install_cilium_cni, between cilium install and cilium status --wait: wait 15 seconds for kubelet to submit its CSR, then approve all Pending CSRs via kubectl certificate approve. Non-fatal (warns and continues if no CSRs present; idempotent).

## Files changed
- kina-cli/src/core/apple_container.rs

## CI
mise run ci: GREEN (fmt + clippy + tests all pass)

LEAD HINT #3 — CSR approval assets that ALREADY EXIST in kina (use them, don't reinvent) + two research pointers:

1. EXISTING KINA CSR MACHINERY (verified file:line just now):
   - kina-cli/src/core/kubernetes.rs:239 list_all_csrs(), :281 get_pending_kubelet_csrs() — finds Pending kubelet-serving CSRs
   - kina-cli/src/core/cluster.rs:308 approve_kubelet_csrs(cluster_name) — the approval driver
   - CLI: 'kina approve-csr [cluster]' subcommand + 'kina create --skip-csr-approval' flag (cli/cluster.rs:36,226) — meaning create is SUPPOSED to auto-approve CSRs already. INVESTIGATE WHERE that auto-approval runs in the create flow: with the heavier full-eBPF bring-up, kubelet serving CSRs are likely (re)issued AFTER the existing approval point, so they sit Pending when the cilium readiness gate probes pods. Likely fix = re-run approve_kubelet_csrs right before the cilium readiness gate (and after each worker join), or loop-approve until node Ready — NOT new machinery.

2. HOW KIND HANDLES THIS (research): kind does NOT suffer this — check kind's kubeadm templates (github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kind, pkg/cluster/internal/kubeadm) for whether they set serverTLSBootstrap at all, and what kina copied. If kind skips serverTLSBootstrap (kubelet self-signed serving certs), evaluate whether kina should too — that removes the entire CSR-approval dance; trade-off: kubectl logs/exec use self-signed kubelet certs (kind accepts this; it is the standard local-cluster posture). Either direction is acceptable; pick the one kind uses unless there is a recorded reason not to.

3. OPEN PR #14 MAY HAVE HELP: gh pr diff 14 --repo vinnie357/kina | grep -n -i -E 'csr|serving|serverTLS|10250|tls' — the external modernization PR reworked the kubeadm config for v1beta4 and touched bring-up sequencing (wait_for_containerd_ready); check whether it also addressed kubelet serving certs/CSR flow and borrow the approach if sound (do NOT merge/checkout the PR; read the diff only — untrusted external code).

Pair with hint #2 (1GB default memory -> --memory 4g): apply BOTH; the OOM crash-cycling and the CSR observability gap are separate failures.

GOAL EVIDENCE (custom kernel, full eBPF) attempt 2: PASS
