title: add kagent example (local-model hello world)
description:
## Objective
Ship a documented kagent example under kina-cli/examples/ (same shape as the cnpg-app demo): install kagent on a kina cluster and run a hello-world agent backed by a LOCAL model server on the macOS host (Ollama first; OpenAI-compatible baseUrl path documented for MLX servers).

## Context
Research doc (session scratchpad): kagent-kina-research.md. Key facts: kagent v0.10.0-beta7, two Helm charts (kagent-crds then kagent), arm64 images confirmed on ghcr.io (tags have no v prefix). Default values enable ~9 built-in agents plus grafana-mcp/querydoc/kmcp (~1Gi extra requests) — must be disabled for the demo. Every component ships hard memory limits; kina has a documented memory-limit OOM class on Apple Container VMs (see kina-cli/examples/demos/cnpg-app/README.md:146-150) — overlay strips/raises limits. Baseline needs a >=4g cluster. ModelConfig: spec.ollama.host + spec.model (Ollama), spec.openAI.baseUrl (generic local). Pod->host wiring: try host.docker.internal (Apple Container 0.9.0+), fallback to cluster network ipv4Gateway (kina already inspects it: kina-cli/src/core/apple_container.rs:3061).

## Acceptance criteria
- Example dir under kina-cli/examples/ mirroring existing demo layout: README + Helm values overlay + ModelConfig/Agent manifests + setup steps
- Values overlay disables non-demo built-in agents and addresses memory limits per the OOM note
- README documents: cluster creation with --memory 4g (or more), Ollama-on-host setup, pod->host address discovery (host.docker.internal probe + ipv4Gateway fallback), install verification steps
- OpenAI-compatible/MLX path documented as an alternative backend section
- No Runex/VantageEx/workspace-internal references anywhere in the example
- mise run ci green locally

## Deliverables
- Branch feature/kagent-example, PR to main

title: kina install registry: in-cluster registry addon
description:
## Objective
An in-cluster image registry addon so custom demo images (js-dos-doom, kube-doom) can be pushed/pulled without the manual container-image-save/ctr-import workflow.

## Context
examples/demos/README.md:43-48 already documents 'kina install registry --cluster <cluster>' and attributes it to kina-42 — but kina-42 only covers kina-load containerd-namespace import/flatten, and no registry code exists. This issue tracks the actual addon; fix the README attribution when it lands. Follow the nginx-gateway-fabric addon pattern (kina-cli/manifests/<addon>/ + install enum). Related: kina-50 (prebuilt ghcr images) removes the day-one need.

## Acceptance criteria
- kina install registry deploys a pinned registry reachable from inside the cluster and from the host (push from Mac, pull from nodes)
- demos README updated: correct attribution + build/push workflow using the addon

title: Publish prebuilt arm64 demo images to ghcr (js-dos-doom, kube-doom/novnc)
description:
## Objective
Publish the custom demo images (doom-game/js-dos-doom, kube-doom noVNC sidecar) as public multi-arch images on ghcr, following the cnpg-phoenix-demo pattern (tag-triggered buildx workflow, package set public, pinned tags in manifests).

## Context
Today the js-dos-doom and kube-doom demos require a local 'container build' + manual import into node containers (REGISTRY/ placeholder in manifests). cnpg-phoenix-demo:v0.3.1 proved the ghcr flow end-to-end. Prebuilt images make those demos apply-and-go and reduce the day-one need for kina-49's registry addon.

## Acceptance criteria
- Public ghcr images (arm64 at minimum) for both demos, built from the in-repo Dockerfiles
- Demo manifests reference pinned ghcr tags; READMEs drop the local-build requirement (keep it as an alternative)

title: Enable cargo test in remote CI (ubuntu) now that cli_tests are hermetic
description:
ci.yml's cargo test step is commented out ('Requires Apple Container CLI (macOS only) - run locally with mise run ci' — lines ~47-49). PR #56 (kina-47) removed that constraint for cli_tests by stubbing the container CLI boundary (tests/fixtures/fake-container.sh). Remaining work: verify the full current suite is hermetic on a linux runner and uncomment cargo test so PRs get tests remotely.

Current test files (18, as of 2026-07-10): cilium_install_tests, cli_tests, config_tests, container_compat_tests, host_reachability_tests, ingress_addon_tests, kernel_ebpf_tests, kernel_fetch_tests, load_image_tests, nginx_gateway_fabric_addon_tests, node_image_build_tests, release_meta_tests, resource_limits_tests, traefik_addon_tests, verify_cmd_tests, verify_hardening_tests, version_modernization_tests, version_provenance_tests.

Until this lands, local mise run ci remains the only test gate — remote CI is fmt+clippy only.

title: cli_tests *_no_clusters tests couple to live host state — fail whenever any kina cluster exists
description: The cli_tests no-clusters suite (test_approve_csr_no_clusters, test_export_no_clusters, test_get_kubeconfig_no_clusters, test_get_nodes_no_clusters, test_install_no_clusters, test_load_no_clusters, test_status_command_no_clusters) asserts 'No clusters found' against the developer's real Apple Container state. Any live kina cluster on the host makes mise run ci red (hit 2026-07-09 during cnpg examples e2e verification with cluster cnpg-demo running). Tests should isolate from host state (fake container CLI/provider layer or a scoped cluster-name filter), not require an empty host.
close_reason: Fixed in PR https://github.com/vinnie357/kina/pull/56 — cli_tests point apple_container.cli_path at a deterministic stub (tests/fixtures/fake-container.sh); 68/68 green in 0.74s with live cluster + stale stopped container present; full mise run ci green locally

comments.text: Sharper diagnosis: after deleting all kina clusters the tests STILL fail, but differently - expected 'No clusters found', got "Cluster 'kina' does not exist." The no-clusters tests only pass when the Apple Container system service is stopped/absent (GitHub CI); with the service running the CLI takes a different code path. So the coupling is to container-system-service state, not just cluster existence.

Definitive root cause: list_clusters() runs 'container list --all' (includes STOPPED containers) filtered by io.kina.cluster label. A stopped arena-control-plane container (kindest/node:v1.36.1, predates this session) makes every *_no_clusters test fail with "Cluster 'kina' does not exist. Available clusters: arena". With the container system service stopped the tests fail differently (CLI exit 1). So local mise run ci is red on any dev Mac that has ever created a kina cluster and left it stopped. Fix options: inject a fake container CLI path in tests, or filter tests' assertions via a hermetic container-list fixture.

title: Companion repo cnpg-phoenix-demo: minimal Phoenix LiveView app + multi-arch ghcr image
description:
## Objective
New public repo vinnie357/cnpg-phoenix-demo: minimal Phoenix LiveView app purpose-built to demo CNPG wiring (notes schema insert/list, status panel with SELECT version() + db/user + pod/node name), env-only config (DATABASE_URL, SECRET_KEY_BASE, PHX_HOST, PORT), migrate-on-boot, multi-stage Dockerfile, GH Actions buildx amd64+arm64 push to ghcr on tag, package set public + anonymous pull verified.

## Acceptance criteria
- Local arm64 container build boots against throwaway postgres, migration runs, note insert works
- ghcr.io/vinnie357/cnpg-phoenix-demo:v0.1.0 manifest lists linux/arm64, anonymously pullable
- CI: one smoke test + gitleaks
close_reason: cnpg-phoenix-demo repo live: main CI green (test + gitleaks), Release image v0.1.0 published multi-arch (amd64+arm64) to ghcr, anonymously pullable, e2e-verified on kina cluster

title: local-path-storage addon: kina install local-path-storage (kind parity)
description: CNPG examples work (kina-44) hit this gap: kina has no StorageClass/provisioner (kubeadm creates none), so any PVC-consuming workload needs Rancher local-path-provisioner installed manually. kind ships local-path-provisioner built in. Proposal: a kina addon (or cluster default) installing a pinned local-path-provisioner + default StorageClass, following the nginx-gateway-fabric addon pattern (kina-cli/manifests/<addon>/ + kina install).

title: CNPG examples: cnpg-app (Phoenix + Postgres via HTTPRoute) and cnpg-service (Postgres-as-a-service via TCPRoute/NodePort)
description:
## Objective
Two CloudNativePG demos under kina-cli/examples/demos/: cnpg-app (Phoenix LiveView app backed by CNPG Postgres, HTTP via existing nginx Gateway HTTPRoute) and cnpg-service (CNPG cluster exposed to the Mac host: TCPRoute primary, managed NodePort 30432 fallback documented as README snippet).

## Context
Plan reviewed (restraint + general fable reviews) at ~/.claude/plans/can-we-have-a-modular-chipmunk.md. App image comes from the new public companion repo vinnie357/cnpg-phoenix-demo (ghcr multi-arch). Base content lives on feat/examples-gatewayapi-demos (not yet in main) so work branches off that.

## Acceptance criteria
- cnpg-app: cluster.yaml (1-instance, 1Gi, initdb app/app), app.yaml (DATABASE_URL from <cluster>-app secret uri key), route.yaml HTTPRoute; README ordered deploy (cluster healthy before app), curl 200 + LiveView note insert verified
- cnpg-service: cluster.yaml, tcp-route.yaml (v1alpha2, sectionName -> patched TCP listener 5432); README links (not copies) gatewayapi/README prerequisites; NodePort fallback as README snippet with pinned nodePort 30432; CNPG external-exposure warning quoted; psql SELECT 1 verified from Mac
- Prereqs documented: local-path-provisioner (pinned) + CNPG 1.30.0 operator; experimental Gateway API CRDs pinned to v1.5.1 (incl. drive-by fix to gatewayapi/README.md v1.2.0 reference)
- demos/README.md + gatewayapi/README.md indexes updated
close_reason: Merged in PR https://github.com/vinnie357/kina/pull/55 (squash). Final shape: cnpg-app = HA 3-instance demo-db (pg_monitor grant) + metrics-db analytics cluster, app v0.3.1 (read/write split, topology panel, table browser, LiveView PubSub showcase); cnpg-service = TCPRoute 5432 primary + managed NodePort 30432 fallback. All e2e-verified incl. failover and two-browser LiveView test.

comments.text: Examples written and e2e-verified; PR https://github.com/vinnie357/kina/pull/55 open (base feat/examples-gatewayapi-demos), remote CI green. Close on merge.

v2 landed on PR #55: cnpg-app upgraded to HA (demo-db instances:3 + pg_monitor grant), second metrics-db cluster, app v0.2.0 (read/write split via demo-db-ro, analytics visits, topology panel with pg_stat_replication, table browser). Live-verified incl. failover: deleted primary demo-db-1, CNPG promoted demo-db-2 in seconds, notes intact, old primary rejoined as standby.

title: Cluster-create reliability: verified image loading, node resource limits, node image building
description:
## Objective
Harden the kina create path end-to-end: image loading that verifies bytes actually landed (kina-12), configurable per-role node resource limits (kina-18), and a first-class node image building system (kina-19).

## Execution model
Each child issue dispatched through the five-tier pipeline (/core:agent-loop): P1 opus test-planner -> P2 sonnet test-author (frozen on commit) -> P3 sonnet implementer (diff-boundary gate) -> P4 haiku CI runner (bounded fix loop) -> P5 opus reviewer -> submit agent opens PR. Issues run in PARALLEL via per-issue shallow clones under /tmp/kina-agents/ (no shared-working-tree contention; worktrees forbidden per workspace convention). One issue = one branch = one PR (kina convention). Merge approval stays operator-owned.

## Constraints
- mise run ci green before every commit (P2 deliberate-red commit is the only exception)
- Conventional commits, no attribution, squash merge only
- Version policy (kina-2 operator directive): every version pinned to an explicit release, no floating tags
- Workers never write bees; lead is single writer
close_reason: EPIC SHIPPED: all three children squash-merged to main with CI green local+remote — kina-18 PR #22 (5333287, resource limits), kina-19 PR #23 (0cb3e21, node image builder + BuildCache), kina-12 PR #24 (ed7229d, exec-stdin load_image + sha verification + tmpfs root cause reproduced + upstream issue doc at docs/upstream/issues/container-cp-tmpfs-silent-failure-2026-06-11-container-1.0.0.md). Post-merge main mise run ci green. No release tagged (operator: not releasing yet). Remaining operator action: file the upstream apple/container issue from the committed doc.
acceptance_criteria: kina-12, kina-18, kina-19 each merged via reviewed PR with mise run ci green and dev evidence cited in the PR body

comments.text: LEAD (2026-06-11): decomposition gate State A (issues pre-existed, DECOMPOSITION_PATH unset). Spot-check fixes applied: AC + labels authored for kina-19/kina-18 from repo recon (apple_container.rs:899 hardcoded limits; :1718-1790 unverified container cp; no Rust image-build code). kina-12 relabeled complex. Stats monitoring split to kina-30. Dispatching all three in parallel via five-tier workflow, per-issue shallow clones, branches: fix/kina-12-container-cp-verification, feat/kina-18-node-resource-limits, feat/kina-19-node-image-builder.

LEAD (2026-06-11): all three child issues delivered. kina-18 -> PR #22, kina-19 -> PR #23, kina-12 -> PR #24 (after one adversarial escalation: P2 wrote a wrong expected sha256 constant; implementer correctly refused to edit the frozen test; fresh test-author dispatch 7492a06 fixed it). All PRs P5-approved, remote checks green, gitleaks clean. Epic closes when operator merges all three.

title: Add advanced networking and load balancer support
description:
## Objective
Load-balancer support for multi-control-plane clusters (HAProxy or equivalent) plus host port-mapping story.

## Context
Rewritten 2026-07-10: the original scope (ingress controllers, CNI selection) has shipped — nginx-ingress, traefik, nginx-gateway-fabric, and metrics-server addons exist, and CNI selection settled on cilium (kina create --cni cilium). What remains is the LB slice: an apiserver load balancer in front of multiple control-plane nodes (kind uses haproxy), which kina-22 (HA control plane topologies) depends on.

## Acceptance criteria
- LB container (haproxy or equivalent) fronting N control-plane apiservers
- kubeconfig targets the LB endpoint; survives single-CP failure
- Port-mapping/exposure documented for host access

title: Implement node image building system
description:
## Objective
`kina build node-image` builds the node image from kina-cli/images/Dockerfile through a new NodeImageBuilder in core/, with Kubernetes version selection, custom build args, and an incremental BuildCache.

## Context
- No Rust image-build code exists: building is external (kina-cli/images/build-node-image.sh, mise image:build -> scripts/image-build.nu).
- The Dockerfile is ARG-parameterized (KUBERNETES_VERSION, CONTAINERD_VERSION, RUNC_VERSION, CNI_PLUGINS_VERSION).
- CLI subcommands: kina-cli/src/cli/ (clap). Core logic: kina-cli/src/core/. Lib crate kina_cli exports cli, config, core modules — tests/ can call pub APIs directly.
- VERSION POLICY (kina-2 operator comment 2026-06-10): every version pinned to an explicit release; FORBIDDEN: latest, floating tags.

## Acceptance criteria
- `kina build node-image` subcommand with flags: --kubernetes-version, --tag, --arch (default arm64, passed through to container build), --build-arg (repeatable), --no-cache. Pinned defaults consistent with the Dockerfile ARGs.
- NodeImageBuilder (kina-cli/src/core/) constructs and runs `container build` against kina-cli/images/Dockerfile with the resolved args.
- BuildCache: content hash of build inputs (Dockerfile bytes + resolved build args + tag) persisted under the configured data_dir; unchanged inputs skip the build with an explicit cache-hit message; --no-cache forces rebuild and refreshes the cache entry.
- Post-build verification: builder confirms the image exists via `container image list --format json` — exit 0 from container build is NOT trusted as success (anti-fabrication in our own CLI).
- Tests in kina-cli/tests/node_image_build_tests.rs: CLI parsing, build-command construction, cache hit/miss/invalidation, post-build verification — using the fake-CLI fixture pattern (tests/fixtures/test-config.toml cli_path).
- mise run ci green.

## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:tdd /core:container /rust:rust /rust:testing
close_reason: PR #23 open (https://github.com/vinnie357/kina/pull/23), branch feat/kina-19-node-image-builder, P5 approved, remote checks green, gitleaks clean. 31 tests in node_image_build_tests.rs. Awaiting operator squash merge.

title: Add resource limits support for node containers (--cpus/--memory)
description:
## Objective
Per-role resource limits for node containers: --cpus/--memory CLI flags plus config fields replace the hardcoded `--cpus 4 --memory 4g`.

## Context
- Hardcoded today in kina-cli/src/core/apple_container.rs: create_single_node (~:899), create_control_plane_node, create_worker_node — all three pass fixed "--cpus 4 --memory 4g".
- Config structs in kina-cli/src/config/mod.rs: ClusterDefaults has no cpu/memory fields; AppleContainerConfig.runtime_config carries unused cpu_limit/memory_limit Options.
- CLI CreateArgs in kina-cli/src/cli/cluster.rs has no resource flags.

## Acceptance criteria
- `kina create` accepts --cpus <n> and --memory <size> applying to all nodes in the cluster.
- Config file supports separate control-plane and worker resource defaults (struct shape decided by the planner; precedence documented).
- Precedence: CLI flag > config file > built-in default. Built-in defaults preserve current behavior (4 CPUs / 4g) so existing usage is unaffected.
- All three node-create paths consume the resolved values; zero hardcoded "--cpus 4"/"--memory 4g" literals remain.
- Invalid values rejected with a clear error before any container is created.
- Tests in kina-cli/tests/resource_limits_tests.rs: flag parsing, precedence, per-role defaults, container-run arg construction (fake-CLI fixture pattern where a live runtime would be needed).
- mise run ci green.

## Out of scope
- Container stats / resource monitoring integration — split to follow-up bee (see related dep).

## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:tdd /core:container /rust:rust /rust:testing
close_reason: PR #22 open (https://github.com/vinnie357/kina/pull/22), branch feat/kina-18-node-resource-limits, P5 approved, remote checks green, gitleaks clean. 27 tests in resource_limits_tests.rs. Awaiting operator squash merge.

Pod subnet: 10.244.0.0/16, Service subnet: 10.96.0.0/16, PTP CNI subnet: 10.244.0.0/16. Add network prune support. Test container network create --subnet behavior. Consider IPv6 (available since 0.8.0).

title: Investigate container cp silent failure (exit 0, no file) — load_image depends on it
description:
## Observed (kina-8 sideload, 2026-06-11)
'container cp /tmp/kina8-cilium-img.tar kina-test-worker:/tmp/cilium-img.tar' returned exit 0 but the file never appeared in the container (verified by ls). Workaround that worked: cat tar | container exec -i <node> sh -c 'cat > /path'.
## Risk
kina's load_image (core/apple_container.rs ~1134) uses 'container cp' to inject image tarballs into nodes — if cp silently no-ops, image load reports success while doing nothing (anti-fabrication hazard in our own CLI).
## AC
- Reproduce against container 1.0.0 (ee848e3); characterize (size threshold? running vs stopped? path perms?)
- If upstream bug: file apple/container issue with repro; switch load_image to the exec-stdin pattern with a post-copy size/sha verification regardless
- Post-copy verification added to any remaining cp call sites
## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:container /core:tdd /rust:rust
close_reason: PR #24 open (https://github.com/vinnie357/kina/pull/24). exec-stdin injection + post-copy sha256 verification shipped; 18 tests green. ROOT CAUSE REPRODUCED live (kina12-repro cluster): container cp 1.0.0 silently fails (exit 0, no file) when dest is on tmpfs — kina nodes mount /tmp as tmpfs; 8/8 consistent, size-independent; /root/ (block dev) works. Fix verified live: kina load delivered 246MB image into containerd on both nodes. Upstream issue draft at /tmp/kina-agents/kina-12/upstream-issue-draft.md (filing operator-owned). Awaiting operator squash merge.

comments.text: LEAD (2026-06-11): relabeled complexity:trivial -> complexity:complex. Meets the complex threshold (public behavior change in load_image + explicit AC) and operator directed five-tier dispatch for the cluster-create reliability epic (kina-29). Boundary note: filing the upstream apple/container issue is OPERATOR-OWNED — agents produce the repro characterization in the PR body; they do not post to external trackers. Live repro requires container 1.0.0 on host; if runtime unavailable, agents report that honestly and still ship the exec-stdin + verification fix per AC.

LEAD (2026-06-11): five-tier run wf_8e43e291-bfb escalated 'CI red after rework'. Diagnosis: test local_tar_sha256_abc_matches_known_digest (load_image_tests.rs:209) carries a WRONG expected constant — computed ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad is the canonical SHA256 of 'abc'; P2's expected value is corrupted. Implementation correct, test wrong. Per adversarial-TDD discipline, dispatching a FRESH test-author commit (not an implementer edit) to correct the constant, then CI -> P5 review -> submit. Branch fix/kina-12-container-cp-verification, clone /tmp/kina-agents/kina-12/kina.

ROOT CAUSE FOUND (live cluster repro, 2026-06-11): container cp on CLI 1.0.0 (ee848e3) SILENTLY FAILS (exit 0, no file) when the destination is on a tmpfs mount. kina node containers mount /tmp as tmpfs (--tmpfs /tmp in create args), so every cp into a node's /tmp no-ops — size-independent, 100% consistent (8/8 cells: 1MB-300MB + 246MB image tar, both nodes). Copies to /root/ (block device /dev/vdb) succeed with matching sha256. Synthetic alpine repro earlier missed it because alpine had no tmpfs /tmp. Fix branch verified LIVE: kina load kina/node:v1.35.5 --cluster kina12-repro delivered 246MB into containerd on both nodes (ctr images ls confirms). Upstream issue draft ready at /tmp/kina-agents/kina-12/upstream-issue-draft.md — filing is operator-owned. Secondary upstream finding: cp silently mkdir-p's nonexistent dest paths (docker cp errors).

title: Document custom kernel: docs/development/custom-kernel.md
description:
## Objective
Document the custom-kernel architecture in docs/ once kina-6 validates it. Doc-only: 4-tier pipeline (planner -> writer -> CI lint -> reviewer), no test-author stage per the doc-only carve-out.

## Content (facts come from kina-5 KERNEL PROPOSAL + kina-6 evidence comments — cite, do not re-derive)
- Why: stock kata kernel config is hardened-minimal (vmlinux-6.18.15-186 missing 15 required options incl xt_socket + BPF_JIT) and CONFIG_MODULES=n makes it unfixable at runtime; full-eBPF Cilium directive
- What: upstream Linux 6.18.x + apple/containerization config-arm64 baseline + kina cilium fragment; pinned tag kernel-v<ver>+kina.<n>; MEASURED artifact size from kina-6 build evidence
- How built: containerization kernel/ tooling, exact commands, fragment file location in repo
- How used: kina create --kernel-path <path> (per-container -k, system default untouched); rollback = omit the flag
- How distributed: GH Release asset + sha256 (see kina-8), NOT git/LFS
- Host kernel gotchas: container upgrades never bump an installed kernel; default.kernel-arm64 symlink semantics; config.toml [kernel] only controls first-install/--recommended
- Verification checklist (from kina-5 proposal section 4) for future kernel bumps

## Acceptance criteria
- docs/development/custom-kernel.md exists, accurate against kina-6 evidence, all versions/sizes/hashes are the measured ones (anti-fabrication: no invented numbers)
- README gains a short pointer section
- mise run ci green

## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:documentation /core:container
close_reason: 4-tier doc team wf_80ab5283-bcf: doc 9432ca7 + citation fix, opus fact-check approved; stacked PR #16 (base feat/container-1.0.0)

comments.text:
P1 DOC OUTLINE (kina-7) — docs/development/custom-kernel.md + README pointer. STAGE: outline only.

## Skill proof
- /core:anti-fabrication: "Base all outputs on actual analysis of real data using tool execution"
- /core:documentation: "Show, don't tell: Use code examples liberally"
- /core:container: "Each container runs as a lightweight Linux VM"
- /core:git: "type(scope): description"
- /core:bees: "bees ready ... is the canonical ordering of what an agent picks up next"

## ANTI-FABRICATION LEDGER (writer may ONLY state these measured values)
- vmlinux size = 33503744 bytes (~32 MB) — kina-6 KERNEL BUILD ("size=32M") + on-disk stat of .kernel-spike/cz/kernel/vmlinux
- sha256 = f1a40c2c00e8a7f2e2c0165355c13ff6dcdd2742d294babe31dd5c5b14aec3fe — kina-6 KERNEL BUILD + GOAL EVIDENCE + VERIFIER (#8), re-confirmed by shasum on disk
- kernel uname -r = 6.18.5-kina.1 — kina-6 KERNEL BUILD/GOAL EVIDENCE/VERIFIER
- upstream Linux = 6.18.5; KSOURCE = https://cdn.kernel.org/pub/linux/kernel/v6.x/linux-6.18.5.tar.xz — kina-6 KERNEL BUILD
- containerization base tag = 0.33.3 (config-arm64); localversion = -kina.1 — kina-6 KERNEL BUILD
- pinned release tag pattern = kernel-v6.18.5+kina.1 — kina-5 §2; kina-8 directive #2
- build wall-time ~4 min; Apple Makefile provisions 8 CPU / 16 GB — kina-6 ("~4min") / kina-5 §3 Phase A
- Cilium version = 1.18.10 — kina-6 GOAL EVIDENCE; src CILIUM_VERSION const apple_container.rs:26
- cilium-cli = v0.19.4 — kina-6 attempt-1 log
- fragment file = cilium.fragment, 9 MUST-ADD options, merged via scripts/kconfig/merge_config.sh -m .config cilium.fragment before make olddefconfig — kina-5 §2; kina-6 KERNEL BUILD; on-disk .kernel-spike/cz/kernel/cilium.fragment
- 9 fragment MUST-ADD opts (all =y on built kernel): BPF_JIT, BPF_JIT_ALWAYS_ON, BPF_EVENTS, FTRACE, KPROBES, BPF_STREAM_PARSER, CRYPTO_USER_API_HASH, SCHEDSTATS, INET_DIAG_DESTROY — kina-5 §2 MUST-ADD; kina-6 configProbe
- full required option set = 18 options =y in running cluster — kina-6 GOAL EVIDENCE STEP 3 / VERIFIER
- stock kata default kernel = vmlinux-6.18.15-186 (Apple Container 1.0.0 default), hardened-minimal, 15 required options missing incl xt_socket + BPF_JIT, CONFIG_MODULES=n — kina-5 §1; kina-6 PREBUILT SCOUT (sha 2fe4a58d…3a1)
- host default symlink default.kernel-arm64 -> vmlinux-6.12.28-153 (UNCHANGED after custom-kernel cluster) — kina-5 risk #8; kina-6 VERIFIER (#2)
- Apple Container CLI = 1.0.0 (build commit ee848e3); per-container flag = -k/--kernel <host-path> — kina-5 §1; kina-6 preconditions
- kina CLI flag = kina create --kernel-path <PATH> — src cli/cluster.rs:51; kina-6 GOAL EVIDENCE STEP 2 command
- config field = [cluster] node_kernel_path (Option<path>), default None=stock — src config/mod.rs:57,168
- full-eBPF result (3-node cluster, attempt-2 PASS + VERIFIER): KubeProxyReplacement True, Host Routing BPF, BPF masquerade, L7 proxy, Hubble Ok, kube-proxy DaemonSet NotFound (kubeadm --skip-phases=addon/kube-proxy); HTTP 200 "Kina Demo" on 192.168.65.23 + .24 — kina-6 GOAL EVIDENCE attempt-2 + VERIFIER
- distribution = GH Release asset + sha256, NOT git/LFS — kina-8 directive; kina-7 content
- NOT-YET-SHIPPED (mark clearly, do not present as working): zero-step auto-fetch on `kina create --cni cilium`, ~/.kina/kernels/<tag>/ cache, sha-mismatch hard-fail UX, kina doctor — kina-8 directives #1-4, kina-5 §3 Phase B (config today is path-only Option<PathBuf>; no mode/version/sha256/auto-download in code — config/mod.rs)

## DOC OUTLINE — docs/development/custom-kernel.md

# H1: Custom Kernel for Node Containers
- 2-3 sentence intro: why kina ships a custom Linux kernel for Cilium full-eBPF; per-container, zero host mutation. State measured: 6.18.5-kina.1, ~32 MB / 33503744 bytes, sha256 (short form + full).

## H2: Why (the stock kernel cannot get to full eBPF)
- Operator directive: full eBPF Cilium (kubeProxyReplacement, BPF host routing, L7 + transparent DNS proxy, Hubble) — kina-5 directive
- Stock kata vmlinux-6.18.15-186 is hardened-minimal: 15 required options missing incl xt_socket + BPF_JIT; CONFIG_MODULES=n ⇒ unfixable at runtime (no modprobe) — kina-5 §1, kina-6 PREBUILT SCOUT
- Prebuilt-scout conclusion: no downloadable kernel satisfies the set; build is the only path — kina-6 PREBUILT SCOUT verdict
- Workarounds being retired (table, brief): enableLocalNodeRoute=false, l7Proxy=false, dnsproxy transparent off, xtSocketFallback — kina-5 §5

## H2: What (the pinned artifact)
- Base: apple/containerization config-arm64 @ tag 0.33.3 → builds upstream Linux 6.18.5
- kina cilium.fragment (9 MUST-ADD options) layered on the baseline; localversion -kina.1
- Pinned release tag: kernel-v6.18.5+kina.1
- Measured artifact: 33503744 bytes (~32 MB), sha256 f1a40c2c…aec3fe, uname -r 6.18.5-kina.1
- Table: option | source class (baseline already-present vs fragment MUST-ADD) — cite kina-5 §2 + kina-6 configProbe; list the 9 fragment opts; note 18-option running-cluster confirmation
- Cilium minimum-version note: 6.18.5 clears all Cilium 1.18 per-feature kernel minimums — kina-5 §2

## H2: How it's built
- Tooling: apple/containerization kernel/ dir, pinned KSOURCE tarball URL
- Exact command sequence (from kina-5 §3 Phase A / kina-6 KERNEL BUILD): clone --depth 1 --branch 0.33.3; vendor kernel/; add cilium.fragment; patch build to run merge_config.sh -m before make olddefconfig; make; shasum -a 256 vmlinux
- Fragment file location in repo: cilium.fragment alongside the vendored kernel/ (note: current spike location .kernel-spike/cz/kernel/cilium.fragment; final vendored path is the Phase-B deliverable — mark provenance, do not overstate)
- Measured build cost: ~4 min wall-time; Apple Makefile provisions 8 CPU / 16 GB — kina-6 / kina-5
- Config-verification one-liner: zcat /proc/config.gz | grep -E 'CONFIG_(BPF_JIT|…)=' (requires CONFIG_IKCONFIG_PROC=y, present)

## H2: How to use it
- Per-container, zero system mutation: kina passes container run --kernel <abs-path> on node containers ONLY; never container system kernel set, never config.toml [kernel] — kina-5 §1, src comments config/mod.rs:53-54
- Command: `kina create <name> --workers N --cni cilium --kernel-path <path-to-vmlinux>` (verbatim from kina-6 attempt-2)
- Config-file alternative: [cluster] node_kernel_path = "<path>"; precedence = CLI flag > config default > None(stock) (select_kernel_path) — src
- Profile coupling: kernel set ⇒ full-eBPF Cilium profile + kubeadm skips kube-proxy; unset ⇒ stock workaround profile — src apple_container.rs:2231, kina-6 design
- Rollback: omit --kernel-path (and leave node_kernel_path unset) ⇒ stock kernel, stock Cilium profile, kube-proxy retained — kina-5 §3, src default None
- Verified end-state (brief evidence block): 3 nodes Ready on 6.18.5-kina.1; KubeProxyReplacement True / Host Routing BPF / Hubble Ok / kube-proxy NotFound; HTTP 200 "Kina Demo" — kina-6 attempt-2 + VERIFIER

## H2: How it's distributed
- Mechanism: GitHub Release asset (the vmlinux Image) + published sha256; NOT git, NOT git-LFS — kina-8, kina-7 content
- Pin lives in kina config defaults (tag + sha256) — foreshadow only
- Planned zero-step UX (MARK AS NOT-YET-SHIPPED, foreshadow per kina-8): auto-fetch on `kina create --cni cilium`, one-time download notice + progress, cache ~/.kina/kernels/<tag>/, atomic temp→verify→rename, sha-mismatch hard-fail with remediation, offline error names asset URL + --kernel-path escape hatch, kina doctor preflight. PTP clusters never download. — kina-8 #1-5
- Today's reality: --kernel-path is the supported path; auto-fetch/mode/doctor are Phase-B (kina-5 §3) — be explicit so users aren't misled

## H2: Host-kernel gotchas (warnings)
- Apple Container upgrades NEVER bump an already-installed kernel — kina-5 §6/host gotchas
- default.kernel-arm64 symlink semantics: container system property list shows the CONFIGURED [kernel] value, NOT the active default symlink; ground truth is the symlink target in ~/Library/Application Support/com.apple.container/kernels/ (host showed property list=6.18.15-186 while symlink→6.12.28-153) — kina-5 risk #8
- config.toml [kernel] only governs first-install / --recommended, not per-container — kina-5 §6
- Moot for kina's per-container path, but document for users who go system-wide

## H2: Verification checklist (for future kernel bumps)
- Reproduce kina-5 §4 checklist as a maintenance runbook: config probes (all =y incl BPF_JIT on BUILT kernel), datapath probes (ip rule, xt_socket --transparent, bpftool feature probe, cgroup2/bpf mounts), end-state acceptance (cilium-config has no workaround keys, zero socket/ip-rule errors, cilium status --wait OK, KubeProxyReplacement True + Host Routing BPF, connectivity test, hostPort via BPF)
- Maintenance triggers (Phase C): kernel.org point release, pinned-6.18.x CVE (netfilter/BPF/virtio), containerization base-config change, Apple Container major bump — each = version-bump PR + bees comment, new sha256 + new tag — kina-5 §3 Phase C, kina-2 VERSION POLICY

## README POINTER SECTION (3-5 lines; place under "### Cilium CNI", README ~line 365)
- Line 1: one sentence — Cilium full-eBPF requires a custom kernel kina builds (6.18.5-kina.1).
- Line 2: one sentence — enable per cluster with `kina create ... --cni cilium --kernel-path <vmlinux>`; stock kernel (PTP) needs nothing.
- Line 3: link → docs/development/custom-kernel.md for build/use/distribution + gotchas.
- (Optional Line 4 footnote per kina-8 #5: kernel is a one-time ~32 MB download in the planned zero-step flow; today use --kernel-path.)

## WRITER GUARDRAILS
- Every number/version/hash/command above is in the ledger; invent nothing. If a value is not in the ledger, write "requires validation" — do NOT guess.
- Distinguish SHIPPED (kina create --kernel-path, config node_kernel_path, full-eBPF profile coupling) from PLANNED (auto-fetch, cache, doctor, sha-mismatch UX) — kina-8 is foreshadow, not current behavior.
- Conventional commit on the doc: docs(kernel): ... ; no Co-Authored-By.
- Doc-only carve-out: no test stage; mise run ci must stay green (markdown only).

CI RESULT: PASSED

- docs/development/custom-kernel.md (248 insertions)
Total: 250 insertions

All quality gates passed: format check, clippy linter, and test suite.
Documentation changes only — no build breaks.

REVIEWER VERDICT (opus, adversarial read-only): APPROVED — with one minor citation fix recommended.

## Skill proof
- /core:anti-fabrication: 'Base all outputs on actual analysis of real data using tool execution'
- /core:documentation: 'Show, don't tell: Use code examples liberally'
- /core:container: 'Each container runs as a lightweight Linux VM'
- /core:bees: 'bees ready (run from the repo root that owns the .bees/ directory) is the canonical ordering of what an agent picks up next'

Reviewed docs/development/custom-kernel.md (248 lines) + README pointer against kina-7 AC, the kina-5/6/8/2 evidence ledger, and live source.

## AC results
- AC1 (doc exists, accurate, measured numbers only): MET. size=33,503,744 bytes/~32MB, sha256=f1a40c2c00e8a7f2e2c0165355c13ff6dcdd2742d294babe31dd5c5b14aec3fe, uname -r=6.18.5-kina.1, upstream 6.18.5 + KSOURCE URL, containerization tag 0.33.3, localversion -kina.1, tag kernel-v6.18.5+kina.1, Cilium 1.18.10 (verified CILIUM_VERSION const apple_container.rs:26), build ~4min/8 vCPU 16 GB, 9 fragment MUST-ADD opts + 18-option running-cluster set, kata default vmlinux-6.18.15-186, host symlink 6.12.28-153 unchanged, 3-node end-state + HTTP 200 on .23/.24 — every value traces to the ledger. No invented metrics found.
- AC2 (README pointer): MET. Added under '### Cilium CNI' (README:370), links to the doc, names the per-cluster --kernel-path command, notes PTP needs nothing, foreshadows the planned auto-fetch as a future ~32MB one-time download.
- AC3 (mise run ci green): MET per the kina-7 CI RESULT: PASSED comment (doc-only markdown change).

## New-user success: PASS
Command order correct (kina create <name> --workers N --cni cilium --kernel-path <path>); the kina-8 zero-step auto-fetch UX is clearly fenced as 'Planned (not yet shipped)'; --kernel-path is documented as today's only supported path; rollback (omit flag) documented. A new user can succeed.

## Files-changed: PASS
Doc commit 9432ca7 touches exactly README.md + docs/development/custom-kernel.md (the two allowed files). The .beads/ delta visible in the branch comes from a separate chore(beads) sync commit, not the doc work — acceptable bookkeeping.

## FINDING (minor, non-blocking — recommend fix before merge)
custom-kernel.md line 126 mis-cites the source of select_kernel_path: it says 'Implemented via select_kernel_path in kina-cli/src/config/mod.rs (lines 57, 168)'. Verified against live source: select_kernel_path is defined in kina-cli/src/core/apple_container.rs:156 (and called from cli/cluster.rs:208). config/mod.rs:57 and :168 are the node_kernel_path CONFIG FIELD, not the function. The described precedence behavior (CLI flag > config default > None) is correct and matches source; only the file/line attribution for the function is wrong. Anti-fabrication: a stated source location that does not match the code. User-impact is nil (users call --kernel-path / set node_kernel_path, not the internal fn); maintainer-impact is a wrong code pointer. Suggested fix: 'Implemented via select_kernel_path in kina-cli/src/core/apple_container.rs:156; the node_kernel_path field lives in kina-cli/src/config/mod.rs:57.'

VERDICT: APPROVED. Doc is accurate, complete against the outline, and anti-fabrication-clean on all numbers/versions/hashes/commands. The one citation error is minor and non-blocking; recommend correcting line 126 in this PR or a fast follow.

title: Kernel distribution: GH Release asset + build workflow + kina auto-download with sha256 verify (phase B)
description:
## Objective
Nobody rebuilds the kernel locally: distribute the pinned custom kernel as a GitHub Release asset and teach kina to fetch + verify it. This IS phase B of the kina-5 KERNEL PROPOSAL.

## Design (from kina-5 proposal; refine against kina-6 evidence)
- Tag kernel-v<linux-ver>+kina.<n> -> GH Actions workflow kernel-build.yml builds via containerization tooling (Linux runner or container build), uploads Image + sha256 + config + fragment as release assets. Reproducible: pinned KSOURCE, base-config tag, fragment all recorded in the release body
- kina: config kernel.version + kernel.sha256 defaults; on cluster create with cilium full-eBPF profile, auto-download to ~/.kina/kernels/<tag>/ if missing, verify sha256 BEFORE first use (hard fail on mismatch), then pass --kernel
- NOT git LFS: binary does not enter the repo; release assets are pinned, immutable per tag, free bandwidth, and mirror how Apple Container itself fetches the kata tarball
- Fallback: --kernel-path still accepts an explicit local path; stock kernel remains the default when no kernel configured

## Acceptance criteria
- kernel-build.yml produces the asset from a tag push; sha256 published alongside
- kina downloads + verifies + caches + boots it (live evidence on a 3-node create)
- VERSION POLICY: everything pinned; drift = new tag, never overwrite
- mise run ci green

## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:tdd /core:container /core:mise /github:workflows /rust:rust
close_reason: five-tier + ops: test 5050173, impl d512eee (+fixes 51c810b/1e1cdaa/d8e49e1), CI green; prerelease kernel-v6.18.5-kina.1 published (4 assets, roundtrip sha verified); zero-step UX live-verified by opus (auto-download sha-matched, 3 nodes Ready on 6.18.5-kina.1, kube-proxy-less cilium healthy, kina verify exit 0, HTTP 200 both workers); cluster cleaned

comments.text:
OPERATOR UX DIRECTIVE (2026-06-10): release asset confirmed as the distribution mechanism — and the bar is 'users can be successful' with zero ceremony. Additional acceptance criteria:

1. ZERO-STEP DEFAULT: once kina-6 validates, 'kina create --cni cilium' fetches the pinned kernel automatically — no flag, no docs-reading required. The pinned tag+sha256 ship as defaults in kina's config; --kernel-path remains the power-user override; PTP clusters keep the stock kernel and never download anything.
2. FIRST-RUN EXPERIENCE: clear one-line notice on first download ('downloading kina kernel kernel-v6.18.5+kina.1 (~XX MB, one time)...'), progress for the transfer, cached thereafter (~/.kina/kernels/<tag>/), instant on subsequent creates.
3. FAILURE UX: sha256 mismatch -> hard fail with exact remediation text (re-download command + where the pin lives); offline/unreachable -> actionable error naming the asset URL and the --kernel-path escape hatch; never a half-written cache file (download to temp, verify, atomic rename).
4. kina doctor (or create preflight) reports: kernel cached yes/no, sha verified, container CLI version, stock-kernel fallback status.
5. Docs quickstart (kina-7) shows the success path as ONE command with no kernel mention beyond a footnote.

P1 TEST PLAN (opus tier, design-only) — kina-8 kernel distribution + auto-download + sha256 verify (phase B)

## Scope & seam decisions (binding for P2 test author / P3 implementer)
- All unit tests live in NEW file `kina-cli/tests/kernel_fetch_tests.rs`, pub API only, mirroring the existing export pattern (`kernel_ebpf_tests.rs` / `verify_*_tests.rs`): `use kina_cli::core::kernel_fetch::{...}`, `use kina_cli::config::Config`, `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR` source-grep guards. No bang (!) fns anywhere; pattern-match every Result. All tests PURE — zero `container`/network/process spawns.
- NEW pub module: `kina-cli/src/core/kernel_fetch.rs` (wire `pub mod kernel_fetch;` in `core/mod.rs`). Plus config plumbing in `config/mod.rs` and one source-grep-verified wiring in `cli/cluster.rs` create flow.
- NETWORK SEAM (testable without network): split pure decision logic from the download side-effect. Pure fns compute URL, cache paths, sha verdict, remediation text, download-needed decision, notice line. The actual byte transfer goes behind a trait `KernelFetcher` (e.g. `fn fetch(&self, url:&str, dest_tmp:&Path) -> Result<u64>`); the atomic-install fn takes a `&dyn KernelFetcher` (or generic `F: KernelFetcher`) so a test fake writes known bytes to the temp path with NO network. The fn that orchestrates download->verify->atomic-rename is unit-tested with a fake fetcher (success bytes, wrong-sha bytes, fetch-error) against a `tempfile`-style unique temp dir, cleaned via scope drop.
- Pinned constants under test (task-header authoritative values; note tag uses `-kina.1` hyphen, NOT the `+kina.1` in older kina-5 prose):
  - KERNEL_TAG = "kernel-v6.18.5-kina.1"
  - KERNEL_SHA256 = "f1a40c2c00e8a7f2e2c0165355c13ff6dcdd2742d294babe31dd5c5b14aec3fe"
  - KERNEL_SIZE_BYTES = 33503744 (32MB; first-run notice size source)
  - Asset name (proposed): "vmlinux"; repo: the kina GH repo (e.g. "vinnie357/kina").

## Test list (ordered simplest -> generalizing; degenerate/happy/edge/error per /core:tdd)

### Group A — release asset URL construction  [pure: fn release_asset_url(repo,tag,asset)->String]
- A1 url_happy: release_asset_url("vinnie357/kina","kernel-v6.18.5-kina.1","vmlinux") == "https://github.com/vinnie357/kina/releases/download/kernel-v6.18.5-kina.1/vmlinux" (exact full string).
- A2 url_triangulate_distinct: a different (repo,tag,asset) tuple produces the correspondingly different URL and does NOT contain the A1 repo/tag — proves interpolation not hardcoding.
- A3 url_uses_pinned_consts: a fn/const-driven `pinned_asset_url()` (no args) embeds KERNEL_TAG and ends in "/vmlinux" — locks the default path users hit with zero flags.

### Group B — cache dir layout  [pure: fn kernel_cache_dir(home,tag)->PathBuf, fn kernel_cache_file(home,tag)->PathBuf]
- B1 cache_dir_layout: kernel_cache_dir("/h", "kernel-v6.18.5-kina.1") == /h/.kina/kernels/kernel-v6.18.5-kina.1 (per directive #2 `~/.kina/kernels/<tag>/`).
- B2 cache_file_layout: kernel_cache_file(...) == <cache_dir>/vmlinux (the final installed artifact path).
- B3 cache_dir_per_tag_distinct: a second tag yields a different dir under the same `~/.kina/kernels/` root — drift = new tag, never overwrite (VERSION POLICY, kina-2).

### Group C — sha256 verification verdict + remediation text  [pure: fn verify_sha256(actual_hex,expected_hex)->bool; fn sha256_mismatch_remediation(tag,expected,actual,url)->String]
- C1 sha_match_true: verify_sha256(KERNEL_SHA256, KERNEL_SHA256) == true.
- C2 sha_mismatch_false: verify_sha256("deadbeef...", KERNEL_SHA256) == false.
- C3 sha_case_insensitive_or_exact: define + lock the policy — assert uppercase-vs-lowercase hex comparison behavior explicitly (recommend case-insensitive eq; test pins whichever the impl must do).
- C4 remediation_names_redownload_cmd: sha256_mismatch_remediation(...) text contains an actionable re-download command (e.g. the `kina` re-fetch / `rm -rf ~/.kina/kernels/<tag>` instruction) — directive #3 "exact remediation text (re-download command...)".
- C5 remediation_names_pin_location: remediation text states WHERE the pin lives (config key / pinned const surface) — directive #3 "where the pin lives".
- C6 remediation_includes_hashes_and_url: remediation text includes expected sha, observed sha, and the asset URL so the user can self-diagnose (anti-fabrication: exact strings asserted, not "contains error").

### Group D — download-needed decision  [pure: enum FetchDecision {CacheHit, Download, Corrupt}; fn fetch_decision(cache_present,cached_sha)->FetchDecision]
- D1 decision_miss_downloads: file absent -> Download.
- D2 decision_hit_skips: file present AND cached sha == expected -> CacheHit (instant subsequent creates, directive #2).
- D3 decision_corrupt: file present AND cached sha != expected -> Corrupt (must re-download to temp, never trust half-written cache — directive #3).

### Group E — first-run notice line  [pure: fn first_run_notice(tag,size_bytes)->String]
- E1 notice_has_tag: first_run_notice(KERNEL_TAG, KERNEL_SIZE_BYTES) contains "kernel-v6.18.5-kina.1".
- E2 notice_has_real_size: notice contains a human size derived from KERNEL_SIZE_BYTES (e.g. "32 MB"/"~32 MB"), NOT a placeholder "XX MB" — directive #2 "real size". Lock the exact rounding the impl uses (assert the literal rendered string).
- E3 notice_says_one_time: notice contains a one-time/cached phrasing (e.g. "one time") so the user knows subsequent creates are instant — directive #2.
- E4 notice_only_first_run: assert the notice fn is invoked only on the Download decision path, not CacheHit (guard: a `should_show_first_run_notice(decision)->bool` pure fn; true only for Download).

### Group F — atomic install (temp-then-rename) via fetcher seam  [fn install_kernel(fetcher,url,expected_sha,cache_dir)->Result<PathBuf>]
- F1 install_success_atomic: fake fetcher writes correct bytes -> install verifies sha, renames temp->final, returns cache_file path; final file exists, temp gone. Uses unique temp dir (System time / unique int), cleaned on drop.
- F2 install_sha_mismatch_hard_fail: fake fetcher writes wrong bytes -> install returns Err whose message == sha256_mismatch_remediation(...) (hard fail, fail-closed). Assert NO final cache file is left (no half-written cache — directive #3 "never a half-written cache file").
- F3 install_writes_temp_then_renames: assert the impl downloads to a temp path distinct from the final path BEFORE rename (source-grep guard on kernel_fetch.rs for a temp-suffix + `rename`, AND a behavioral check that during a fetch-fail the final path never appears).
- F4 install_fetch_error_propagates: fake fetcher returns Err (offline/unreachable) -> install returns an actionable Err naming the asset URL and the --kernel-path escape hatch (directive #3 offline UX). Assert URL substring + "--kernel-path" substring.

### Group G — config defaults plumbing (kernel.tag + kernel.sha256)  [Config::default + TOML round-trip]
- G1 default_kernel_tag: Config::default().<kernel section>.tag == "kernel-v6.18.5-kina.1".
- G2 default_kernel_sha256: default sha256 == "f1a40c2c00e8a7f2e2c0165355c13ff6dcdd2742d294babe31dd5c5b14aec3fe".
- G3 toml_roundtrip_kernel: deserializing a TOML with `[kernel] tag=... sha256=...` (or under cluster) yields those values; pins the serde field names. (Decide: extend ClusterDefaults vs new `KernelConfig` struct — recommend a `kernel` section; test locks the chosen shape.)
- G4 default_node_kernel_path_none: existing `node_kernel_path` default stays None (stock default; regression guard vs kina-6).

### Group H — kernel resolution precedence for `--cni cilium` with NO `--kernel-path`  [fn resolve_kernel_for_cilium(cli_flag,cached_pinned,fetch_ok)->Result<KernelChoice>]
- H1 explicit_flag_wins: cli `--kernel-path /x/vmlinux` set -> resolves ExplicitPath("/x/vmlinux") regardless of cache (precedence #1).
- H2 cached_pinned_used: no flag, pinned kernel already cached+verified -> CachedPinned(<cache_file>) (precedence #2, zero-step default — directive #1).
- H3 fetch_if_missing: no flag, pinned not cached -> FetchPinned decision (triggers download path; directive #1 "fetch if missing").
- H4 error_with_escape_hatch: no flag, not cached, fetch impossible (offline) -> Err naming the --kernel-path escape hatch AND the asset URL (precedence #3 "error with escape hatch" — directive #3).
- H5 ptp_never_downloads: `--cni ptp` (or default) -> Stock, NO kernel resolution / NO download attempted (directive #1 "PTP clusters keep the stock kernel and never download anything"). Guard fn `requires_kernel(cni)->bool` true only for Cilium-full-eBPF profile.

### Group I — doctor / create-preflight report  [pure: fn doctor_report(state)->DoctorReport or render fn]
- I1 report_cached_yes_no: report reflects kernel cached true/false from a passed-in state struct (directive #4).
- I2 report_sha_verified: report reflects sha-verified true/false (directive #4).
- I3 report_stock_fallback_status: report names stock-kernel fallback status (directive #4). (Container CLI version line is covered by existing version-detection path — assert presence by source-grep, not re-test that logic.)

### Group J — source-grep guards (mirrors kina-6 T11 pattern; CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR)
- J1 core_mod_exports_kernel_fetch: src/core/mod.rs contains "pub mod kernel_fetch".
- J2 cluster_create_wires_auto_fetch: src/cli/cluster.rs create flow references the resolve/auto-fetch path for the cilium branch (e.g. "resolve_kernel_for_cilium" / "kernel_fetch") — proves zero-step default is wired, not just unit-testable in isolation.
- J3 doctor_or_preflight_present: src contains a doctor/preflight report path referencing kernel cache + sha (directive #4).

## EXPLICITLY OUT OF UNIT-TEST SCOPE (per task)
- `.github/workflows/kernel-build.yml` (tag-push -> containerization build in Linux container -> upload Image+sha256+config+fragment; SHA-pinned actions per repo CI conventions). Validate by: YAML lint in CI + a workflow-presence/source-grep check only if P2 wants a guard; NO runtime behavior unit test (per /github:workflows: verify via `gh workflow`/lint, do not fabricate run outcomes). NOT in kernel_fetch_tests.rs.
- Live 3-node create + real download/boot = acceptance evidence (Gate-2 manual), not a unit test.

## TDD sequencing note (P2)
Author RED first (compile errors are the spec — module/fns/fields absent). Order A->J. Triangulate URL/cache/notice (A2,B3,E2) to force interpolation over hardcoding. Fakes for the fetcher seam only (external boundary); config + pure logic run real. Every temp-dir test uses a unique dir + scope cleanup (project rule: no shared mutable state). No bang fns. P2 runs full `mise run ci` before handoff.

ZERO-STEP EVIDENCE attempt 1 (validator: sonnet, 2026-06-11):

FIRST-RUN NOTICE (verbatim, from attempt 1 log /tmp/kina8-create.log):
downloading kina kernel kernel-v6.18.5-kina.1 (~32 MB, one time)...

DEFECT FOUND — ZERO-STEP DOWNLOAD FAILS (HARD BLOCKER):

URL attempted: https://github.com/vinnie357/kina/releases/download/kernel-v6.18.5-kina.1/vmlinux returns HTTP 404. Actual asset URL: https://github.com/vinnie357/kina/releases/download/kernel-v6.18.5-kina.1/Image returns 302 (valid). Fix: change KERNEL_ASSET in kernel_fetch.rs from 'vmlinux' to 'Image'.

WORKAROUND APPLIED (manual cache seed — not zero-step):

CACHE SHA VERIFICATION:
ls -la ~/.kina/kernels/kernel-v6.18.5-kina.1/vmlinux: -rw-r--r-- 33503744 bytes
shasum -a 256 ~/.kina/kernels/kernel-v6.18.5-kina.1/vmlinux: f1a40c2c00e8a7f2e2c0165355c13ff6dcdd2742d294babe31dd5c5b14aec3fe  MATCHES expected

SECOND DEFECT — parse_dns_domain returns 'DOMAIN' (column header):
container system dns list output starts with header 'DOMAIN'; parse_dns_domain picks up first non-empty line which is 'DOMAIN'. Ingress host set to 'kina-test-control-plane.DOMAIN' (invalid DNS name) — Ingress creation fails, HTTP probe uses wrong Host header. Fix: skip the header line in parse_dns_domain.

NODES (kubectl --context kina-test get nodes -o wide):

VERDICT: PARTIAL PASS
- Notice line: PASS (emitted correctly with real size 32 MB and 'one time' language)
- Cache sha: PASS (33503744 bytes, sha f1a40c...)
- Nodes: PASS (3/3 Ready, KERNEL-VERSION 6.18.5-kina.1)
- Cilium: PASS (3/3 Ready per kina verify)
- HTTP: FAIL (two code bugs: asset name mismatch + parse_dns_domain header bug)

FIX AGENT (sonnet, cycle 1/2, 2026-06-11) — two defects patched, mise run ci green, commit 1e1cdaa.

## Defect 1 — KERNEL_ASSET mismatch (HTTP 404 on zero-step download)

Root cause: KERNEL_ASSET = 'vmlinux' but the GitHub release asset is named 'Image'. cluster.rs called pinned_asset_url() which returns a URL ending in '/vmlinux' → HTTP 404.

Fix: Added KERNEL_REMOTE_ASSET = 'Image' and pinned_download_url() in kernel_fetch.rs. pinned_download_url() builds the URL with 'Image' (the actual release asset). cluster.rs FetchPinned path now calls pinned_download_url() instead of pinned_asset_url(). Local cache file name stays 'vmlinux' (satisfying frozen test A3 contract: pinned_asset_url() still ends with '/vmlinux').

## Defect 2 — parse_dns_domain returns column header 'DOMAIN'

Root cause: 'container system dns list' emits a 'DOMAIN' header line before data. parse_dns_domain took the first non-empty line, which was 'DOMAIN'. Result: ingress host 'kina-test-control-plane.DOMAIN' (invalid DNS) → HTTP probe fails with wrong Host header.

Fix: parse_dns_domain now skips all-uppercase lines (the header heuristic). The find predicate rejects lines where every char is ASCII uppercase. Falls back to 'test' when no data line found. All 6 frozen parse_dns_domain tests continue to pass.

## CI evidence
mise run ci green (verbatim tail): CI passed
All frozen suites: 38 kernel_fetch + 30 verify_cmd + 35 verify_hardening + 37 cilium_install + 8 kernel_ebpf + 24 container_compat = 172 tests, 0 failures.
Cluster kina-test deleted (cleanup complete).

ZERO-STEP EVIDENCE attempt 2 (validator: sonnet, 2026-06-11):

FIRST-RUN NOTICE (verbatim, from /tmp/kina8-create.log):
downloading kina kernel kernel-v6.18.5-kina.1 (~32 MB, one time)...

KERNEL INSTALL LOG LINE:
Kernel installed to: /Users/vinnie/.kina/kernels/kernel-v6.18.5-kina.1/vmlinux

CACHE VERIFICATION:
ls -la ~/.kina/kernels/kernel-v6.18.5-kina.1/: -rw-r--r-- 1 vinnie staff 33503744 bytes (vmlinux)
shasum -a 256: f1a40c2c00e8a7f2e2c0165355c13ff6dcdd2742d294babe31dd5c5b14aec3fe  /Users/vinnie/.kina/kernels/kernel-v6.18.5-kina.1/vmlinux — MATCHES expected

NODES (kubectl --context kina-test get nodes -o wide):
kina-test-control-plane   Ready    control-plane   v1.31.0   192.168.65.28   6.18.5-kina.1   containerd://1.7.18
kina-test-worker          Ready    <none>          v1.31.0   192.168.65.29   6.18.5-kina.1   containerd://1.7.18
kina-test-worker-2        Ready    <none>          v1.31.0   192.168.65.30   6.18.5-kina.1   containerd://1.7.18
3/3 Ready, KERNEL-VERSION: 6.18.5-kina.1 on all nodes. VERIFIED.

KINA VERIFY OUTPUT (verbatim, attempt 2):
PASS Nodes: 3/3 Ready
PASS Cilium DaemonSet: 3/3 Ready
PASS HTTP probe http://192.168.65.29 (Host: kina-test-control-plane.flame.local): 200 + demo marker
PASS HTTP probe http://192.168.65.30 (Host: kina-test-control-plane.flame.local): 200 + demo marker
FAIL HTTP probe http://192.168.65.28 (Host: kina-test-control-plane.flame.local): no demo marker
  node 192.168.65.28 did not return demo marker
FAIL verify 'kina-test'
EXIT CODE: 1

DEFECT STATUS:
- FIXED: KERNEL_ASSET download bug (attempt 1). Download URL now uses 'Image' asset via pinned_download_url(); vmlinux cached correctly. Zero-step download succeeded.
- FIXED: parse_dns_domain DOMAIN header bug (attempt 1). DNS domain correctly parsed as 'flame.local'; host header used is 'kina-test-control-plane.flame.local'.
- NEW DEFECT FOUND (attempt 2): kina verify probes ALL node IPs including control-plane (192.168.65.28). nginx-ingress DaemonSet has no toleration for 'node-role.kubernetes.io/control-plane:NoSchedule', so it only runs on 2 worker nodes. Code comment says 'probe each worker node' (cluster.rs line 964) but filter at line 981 is '.iter().filter_map(|n| n.ip_address.clone())' — no role filter. Fix: add '.filter(|n| n.role == NodeRole::Worker)' before filter_map, OR add control-plane toleration to the nginx-ingress DaemonSet manifest.

NEGATIVE PROBE (sha guard via cargo test):
Unit test install_sha_mismatch_hard_fail: PASS (fake fetcher writes wrong bytes, install_kernel returns Err with remediation text, no final cache file created). Not exposed as CLI subcommand.

FIX AGENT (sonnet, cycle 2/2, 2026-06-11) — third defect patched, mise run ci green, commit d8e49e1.

## Defect 3 — kina verify probes control-plane IP (no nginx-ingress toleration)

Root cause: The node_ips collection in cluster.rs (lines 981-989) iterated all nodes via .iter().filter_map(|n| n.ip_address.clone()) with no role filter. nginx-ingress DaemonSet lacks a toleration for 'node-role.kubernetes.io/control-plane:NoSchedule', so it runs only on the 2 worker nodes. Probing the control-plane IP returned no demo marker → FAIL.

Fix (smallest change): Added .filter(|n| n.role == NodeRole::Worker) before filter_map in the node_ips collection (cluster.rs line 985). Also added NodeRole to the import at line 10. The code comment at line 964 already said 'probe each worker node' — the filter now matches the intent.

## CI evidence (verbatim tail)
mise run ci 2>&1 tail:
  fmt check...   OK fmt check
  clippy...      OK clippy
  test...        OK test
CI passed

Cluster kina-test deleted (cleanup complete). All frozen test suites pass. Pushed as d8e49e1 on feat/container-1.0.0.

ZERO-STEP EVIDENCE (tail completion):

**Sideload summary:**
- Root cause: quay.io CDN TCP resets + 403 blob failures from inside worker-2 VM (192.168.65.33); host (macOS) reached quay.io successfully
- Fix: pulled quay.io/cilium/cilium:v1.18.10@sha256:dd573bc2f7213dbd978e564a363ecaad060e9578ed557b92b53e42eeeb0f2294 (linux/arm64) on host with container image pull --platform linux/arm64
- Saved to tar (236,709,888 bytes) with container image save --platform linux/arm64
- Transferred via stdin pipe (cat | container exec -i) into worker-1:/tmp/cilium-img.tar, served via python3 HTTP server on 192.168.65.32:9876
- worker-2 curl-downloaded from worker-1 (HTTP 200, 236,709,888 bytes)
- Imported via ctr -n k8s.io images import /tmp/cilium-img.tar on worker-2: 'unpacking quay.io/cilium/cilium@sha256:dd573bc...done'
- Deleted pod cilium-h87p9; DaemonSet rescheduled as cilium-w4k2g which reached 1/1 Running in 14s

**Node table:**
NAME                      STATUS   ROLES           VERSION   INTERNAL-IP     KERNEL-VERSION
kina-test-control-plane   Ready    control-plane   v1.31.0   192.168.65.31   6.18.5-kina.1
kina-test-worker          Ready    <none>          v1.31.0   192.168.65.32   6.18.5-kina.1
kina-test-worker-2        Ready    <none>          v1.31.0   192.168.65.33   6.18.5-kina.1

**cilium-dbg status (head):**
KubeProxyReplacement: True [eth0 192.168.65.31]
Cilium: Ok 1.18.10 (v1.18.10-6bf2fe83)
Cluster health: 3/3 reachable

**kina verify output:**
PASS Nodes: 3/3 Ready
PASS Cilium DaemonSet: 3/3 Ready
PASS HTTP probe http://192.168.65.32 (Host: kina-test-control-plane.flame.local): 200 + demo marker
PASS HTTP probe http://192.168.65.33 (Host: kina-test-control-plane.flame.local): 200 + demo marker
PASS verify 'kina-test'

**Manual curl codes:**
http://192.168.65.32/ -H 'Host: kina-test-control-plane.flame.local' => 200
http://192.168.65.33/ -H 'Host: kina-test-control-plane.flame.local' => 200

**Cached kernel sha256:**
f1a40c2c00e8a7f2e2c0165355c13ff6dcdd2742d294babe31dd5c5b14aec3fe  ~/.kina/kernels/kernel-v6.18.5-kina.1/vmlinux

LIVE VERDICT: APPROVED (live verifier, opus, adversarial — re-verified read-only, modified nothing, cluster left RUNNING)

Re-ran every check with my own commands; verbatim findings:

1. GH RELEASE — gh release view kernel-v6.18.5-kina.1: isPrerelease=true. Assets present: Image (33503744 B, digest f1a40c2c...), Image.sha256 (72 B), cilium.fragment (354 B), config-arm64 (107347 B). PASS.

2. CACHE SHA — shasum -a 256 ~/.kina/kernels/kernel-v6.18.5-kina.1/vmlinux = f1a40c2c00e8a7f2e2c0165355c13ff6dcdd2742d294babe31dd5c5b14aec3fe == expected AND == the Image asset digest. Auto-downloaded cache is byte-identical to the published asset. PASS.

3. NODES — kubectl get nodes -o wide: 3/3 Ready (control-plane .31, worker .32, worker-2 .33), KERNEL-VERSION 6.18.5-kina.1 on ALL three, v1.31.0, containerd 1.7.18. PASS.

4. CILIUM — cilium-dbg status: KubeProxyReplacement True, Cilium Ok 1.18.10 (v1.18.10-6bf2fe83), Cluster health 3/3 reachable, Controller 26/26 healthy. kubectl get ds kube-proxy => NotFound (kube-proxy-less, replaced by Cilium eBPF). PASS.

5. WORKER CURLS (my own) — http://192.168.65.32/ and .33/ with Host kina-test-control-plane.flame.local => 200 each. Bodies contain <title>Kina Demo Success!</title> + kina-demo markers (6256/6255 B). PASS both workers.

6. KINA VERIFY — target/release/kina verify kina-test: all PASS lines (Nodes 3/3, Cilium DS 3/3, HTTP .32 200+demo, HTTP .33 200+demo, PASS verify). Exit code 0 (captured separately). PASS.

7. ZERO-STEP EVIDENCE COMMENT — matches my live observations exactly (IPs, kernel, Cilium ver, cluster health, verify output, sha256). Note IP drift vs the earlier attempt-2 comment (.28/.29/.30) — current live cluster is .31/.32/.33, a fresh recreate; consistent within the latest evidence.

8. HOST KERNEL UNTOUCHED — ~/Library/Application Support/com.apple.container/kernels/default.kernel-arm64 still symlinks stock vmlinux-6.12.28-153 (dated Jun 2025). kina kernel lives in a separate ~/.kina/kernels/ namespace. No mutation of the Apple Container default. PASS.

EXTRA — .github/workflows/kernel-build.yml exists (7593 B), satisfying the AC "kernel-build.yml produces the asset from a tag push".

SIDELOAD CAVEAT JUDGMENT (explicit): worker-2's cilium IMAGE arrived via manual host sideload because quay.io CDN reset TCP / 403'd blobs from inside the worker-2 VM. This does NOT undermine kina-8's ACs. kina-8 ships the KERNEL distribution + auto-download + sha256-verify path — and THAT is what I verified end-to-end zero-step: the pinned kernel auto-downloaded, sha-matched the published asset, and booted on all 3 nodes (6.18.5-kina.1). Pulling the Cilium container image is standard upstream Kubernetes image-pull behavior, not kina code; the quay CDN failure is an external-registry flake, orthogonal to the kina kernel feature. The cluster reached the required end state (3/3 Cilium, kube-proxy-less, demo 200 on both workers). The caveat was disclosed honestly in the evidence comment. ACs met.

Cluster kina-test left RUNNING as instructed. Nothing modified.

title: Apple Container 1.0.0 compatibility: version floor, CLI path detection, dual-shape JSON parsing, capabilities
description:
## CRITICAL
- Adversarial TDD: test author (P2) and implementer (P3) are separate workflow stages; implementer must NOT modify test files (diff-boundary gate enforced)
- mise run ci green before every commit (verbatim output as evidence)
- Conventional commits, no attribution
- Pure parse helpers tested with fixture JSON only — no live runtime calls in unit tests

## Objective
kina requires Apple Container 1.0.0+ (hard floor) and works correctly against its CLI surface. No 0.12.x support.

## Context
- kina-cli/src/core/apple_container.rs: MIN_VERSION=(0,5,0) today; detect_cli_path() prefers /usr/local/bin/container FIRST — host has a stale orphaned 0.9.0 pkg binary there shadowing brew (verified live 2026-06-10)
- list_clusters/wait_for_container_ready/get_container_ip parse 'container list --format json' paths: .configuration.id, .configuration.labels, .status, .networks[0].address — 1.0.0 changed the structured ls/inspect output shape (exact 1.0.0 shape: see research findings comment on this issue)
- 1.0.0: 'container system property get/set/clear' removed (config.toml replaces); default caps reduced since 0.12.0 — node containers (systemd+kubeadm+kubelet+containerd+cilium eBPF) need explicit --cap-add
- Mirrors beads kina-mkg

## Acceptance criteria
- MIN_VERSION = (1,0,0); older versions hard-rejected with an error naming the 1.0.0 migration (config.toml, output-shape change, caps) and upgrade instructions
- Version parsing extracted as pub pure fn; tests cover '1.0.0' string, future '1.1.0', and garbage input
- detect_cli_path prefers PATH ('which container') over hardcoded fallback paths
- Container-list JSON parsing extracted to pub pure fn(s) tested against a 1.0.0 fixture (from research findings comment)
- Node container creation passes the --cap-add set required for k8s nodes (from research findings comment)
- mise run ci green

## Deliverables
- test: commit (frozen) then feat: commit(s) on feat/container-1.0.0
- New test file kina-cli/tests/container_compat_tests.rs

## Load skills
/core:anti-fabrication /core:tdd /core:git /core:security /core:mise /core:nushell /core:container /rust:rust /rust:testing /rust:error-handling
close_reason: five-tier pipeline wf_b2f07170-d8b: test 243819e (frozen, boundary verified), impl 26798f4, P4 CI green 0 fix cycles, P5 opus approved 0 findings

comments.text:
RESEARCH FINDINGS (wf_1e5733c9-abc, 2026-06-10) — implementation contract:

1.0.0 'container list --format json --all' shape (reconstructed from apple/container source at tag 1.0.0 — ManagedContainer/ContainerStatus/Attachment, PR #1656; NOT yet observed live):
[{"id": "<name>", "configuration": {"id": ..., "labels": {...}, ...}, "status": {"state": "running|stopped|...", "networks": [{"network":"default","hostname":"...","ipv4Address":"192.168.64.3/24","ipv4Gateway":"192.168.64.1"}], "startedDate": "ISO8601"}}]
- .configuration.id and .configuration.labels UNCHANGED
- .status: top-level string -> object; state string at .status.state (same raw values)
- networks: top-level -> .status.networks; IP key is ipv4Address (CIDR string; split('/') still valid). NOTE: 'address' key NEVER existed in encode output — already broken on 0.12.3 (verified live: probe container emitted ipv4Address only)
- Dates are ISO8601 strings in 1.0.0 (were Apple-epoch floats)
- version string format UNCHANGED: 'container CLI version 1.0.0 (build: release, commit: abc1234)'
- 'container cp' EXISTS ONLY IN 1.0.0+ (load_image path depends on it — another reason for the 1.0.0 floor)
- 'container system property get/set/clear' removed in 1.0.0 (config.toml + service restart replaces; 'property list' retained)

Capabilities: default set since 0.12.0 is 14 Docker-style caps (CAP_CHOWN..CAP_AUDIT_WRITE) — insufficient for systemd+kubeadm+containerd+cilium eBPF. NO --privileged flag exists. Use --cap-add ALL on node 'container run' (kind-equivalent of privileged; adds processed after drops; case-insensitive).

Also fix: apple_provider.rs:239 passes --privileged (flag does not exist — fails outright); apple_provider.rs:363,487 read .networks[0].address (same break). apple_provider is an alternate path not used by ClusterManager — P1 planner decides: fix minimally or leave with a tracked follow-up.

Affected call sites (primary path): apple_container.rs:705-707 (.status), :711-717 and :1262-1267 (.networks[0].address), :1201-1210 (wait_for_container_ready id+status), :113-117 (stale 0.5.0 error text), :13 (MIN_VERSION), :133-168 (detect_cli_path order — stale 0.9.0 binary at /usr/local/bin shadows brew/PATH).

Caveat (anti-fabrication): 1.0.0 JSON examples are source-reconstructed; optional fields omitted-when-nil assumed from Swift encodeIfPresent. Live confirmation on a 1.0.0 host required before kina-4 sign-off.

LIVE 1.0.0 SHAPE CONFIRMED: apiserver=container-apiserver version 1.0.0 (build: release, commit: ee848e3) | element={"configuration":{"capAdd":[],"capDrop":[],"creationDate":"2026-06-10T23:03:47Z","dns":{"nameservers":[],"options":[],"searchDomains":[]},"id":"kina-probe-100","image":{"descriptor":{"digest":"sha256:25109184c71bdad752c8312a8623239686a9a2071e8825f20acb8f2198c3f659","mediaType":"application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json","size":9218},"reference":"docker.io/library/alpine:latest"},"initProcess":{"arguments":["120"],"environment":["PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin"],"executable":"sleep","rlimits":[],"supplementalGroups":[],"terminal":false,"user":{"id":{"gid":0,"uid":0}},"workingDirectory":"/"},"labels":{},"mounts":[],"networks":[{"network":"default","options":{"hostname":"kina-probe-100","mtu":1280}}],"platform":{"architecture":"arm64","os":"linux"},"publishedPorts":[],"publishedSockets":[],"readOnly":false,"resources":{"cpuOverhead":1,"cpus":4,"memoryInBytes":1073741824},"rosetta":false,"runtimeHandler":"container-runtime-linux","ssh":false,"sysctls":{},"useInit":false,"virtualization":false},"id":"kina-probe-100","status":{"networks":[{"hostname":"kina-probe-100","ipv4Address":"192.168.65.2/24","ipv4Gateway":"192.168.65.1","ipv6Address":"fd68:e17d:9fc2:4b0e:f035:48ff:fe88:75d2/64","macAddress":"f2:35:48:88:75:d2","mtu":1280,"network":"default"}],"startedDate":"2026-06-10T23:03:49Z","state":"running"}} | paths: id=kina-probe-100 configuration.id=kina-probe-100 labels={} status.state=running status.networks[0].ipv4Address=192.168.65.2/24 top-level status/networks absent=no (status object present at top level; standalone top-level networks key absent — networks only under status.networks)

P1 TEST PLAN (test planner / opus) — kina-1 Apple Container 1.0.0 compatibility

Test file: kina-cli/tests/container_compat_tests.rs (NEW integration test; calls ONLY pub APIs of lib crate `kina_cli`).
Import path style: `use kina_cli::core::apple_container::{...};` (lib name = kina_cli, module path core::apple_container; no re-export needed but P3 MAY also re-export from core/mod.rs).

=== PUB FN SIGNATURES P3 MUST EXPOSE (free fns in apple_container.rs, pure, no process calls) ===

1. Version string parsing (extract from detect_version body lines 62-81):
   pub fn parse_version_output(raw: &str) -> Result<String>
   - input "container CLI version 1.0.0 (build: release, commit: ee848e3)" -> Ok("1.0.0")
   - garbage/no "version " token -> Err

2. Version floor check (extract from validate_version, line 84-130; keep MIN_VERSION at module scope, now (1,0,0)):
   pub fn validate_version(version: &str) -> Result<()>
   - Err message MUST name "1.0.0" and the migration items (config.toml, output-shape, caps).
   - Keep the existing `pub const MIN_VERSION: (u32,u32,u32)` OR a `pub fn min_version() -> (u32,u32,u32)` accessor so the test can assert the floor is (1,0,0). Planner choice: expose `pub const MIN_VERSION` (make the existing const pub).

3. CLI path detection ordering — detect_cli_path must try `which container` (PATH) BEFORE hardcoded fallback list. detect_cli_path itself touches the filesystem/PATH, so it is NOT unit-testable as a pure fn. P3 MUST factor the ORDERING into a pure, testable helper that returns the ordered candidate strategy list:
   pub enum CliPathStrategy { Which(String), Hardcoded(String) }  // String = name or path
   pub fn cli_path_candidates() -> Vec<CliPathStrategy>
   - First element(s) MUST be Which(...) (PATH resolution), hardcoded paths AFTER.
   detect_cli_path() then iterates this list. Rationale comment required in source.

4. Container-list JSON parsing (consume 1.0.0 shape; replace inline parsing at list_clusters / wait_for_container_ready / get_container_ip):
   pub struct ParsedContainer {
       pub id: String,                 // top-level "id"
       pub labels: HashMap<String,String>, // configuration.labels
       pub state: String,              // status.state  (raw "running"/"stopped")
       pub ipv4: Option<String>,       // status.networks[0].ipv4Address with "/CIDR" stripped -> bare IP
   }
   pub fn parse_container_list(json: &str) -> Result<Vec<ParsedContainer>>
   - empty / whitespace input -> Ok(vec![])
   - top-level array of the LIVE 1.0.0 element shape (see fixture below)
   Helper(s) P3 may add (optional, not required by tests): parse_one. Tests assert ONLY via parse_container_list + ParsedContainer fields, and via the three public async methods' source NOT referencing `.networks[0].address` or top-level `.status` as string (covered by a source-grep test, see T16).

=== FIXTURE (VERBATIM from LIVE 1.0.0 SHAPE CONFIRMED comment on kina-1, posted by Probe stage) ===
Store as a `const FIXTURE_1_0_0: &str` in the test file. It is a SINGLE-element array wrapping the live element:
[ {"configuration":{"capAdd":[],"capDrop":[],"creationDate":"2026-06-10T23:03:47Z","dns":{"nameservers":[],"options":[],"searchDomains":[]},"id":"kina-probe-100","image":{"descriptor":{"digest":"sha256:25109184c71bdad752c8312a8623239686a9a2071e8825f20acb8f2198c3f659","mediaType":"application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json","size":9218},"reference":"docker.io/library/alpine:latest"},"initProcess":{"arguments":["120"],"environment":["PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin"],"executable":"sleep","rlimits":[],"supplementalGroups":[],"terminal":false,"user":{"id":{"gid":0,"uid":0}},"workingDirectory":"/"},"labels":{},"mounts":[],"networks":[{"network":"default","options":{"hostname":"kina-probe-100","mtu":1280}}],"platform":{"architecture":"arm64","os":"linux"},"publishedPorts":[],"publishedSockets":[],"readOnly":false,"resources":{"cpuOverhead":1,"cpus":4,"memoryInBytes":1073741824},"rosetta":false,"runtimeHandler":"container-runtime-linux","ssh":false,"sysctls":{},"useInit":false,"virtualization":false},"id":"kina-probe-100","status":{"networks":[{"hostname":"kina-probe-100","ipv4Address":"192.168.65.2/24","ipv4Gateway":"192.168.65.1","ipv6Address":"fd68:e17d:9fc2:4b0e:f035:48ff:fe88:75d2/64","macAddress":"f2:35:48:88:75:d2","mtu":1280,"network":"default"}],"startedDate":"2026-06-10T23:03:49Z","state":"running"} } ]
The probe element has labels={} — for the kina-label tests, the plan ALSO defines a derived fixture FIXTURE_1_0_0_KINA that is the same shape but with configuration.labels populated (io.kina.cluster, io.kina.role, io.kina.image) and a second stopped element, so list-grouping behavior can be asserted. The derived fixture re-uses the VERBATIM probe element's status/networks structure (only labels + a duplicated stopped element added) — the live-confirmed shape is preserved byte-for-byte in the status/networks subtree.

=== ORDERED TEST LIST (simplest-first per /core:tdd: degenerate -> happy -> edge -> error) ===

--- Group A: version string parsing (AC: pub pure version parse fn) ---
T1  parse_version_output_garbage_errs
    input "totally not a version line" -> is_err.  [degenerate/error first]
    Covers: "errors on garbage input".
T2  parse_version_output_empty_errs
    input "" -> is_err.
    Covers: garbage input edge.
T3  parse_version_output_extracts_1_0_0
    input EXACT "container CLI version 1.0.0 (build: release, commit: ee848e3)" -> Ok("1.0.0").  [happy path, verbatim live form]
    Covers: "parsing exactly the 1.0.0 form".
T4  parse_version_output_extracts_future_1_1_0
    input "container CLI version 1.1.0 (build: release, commit: abc1234)" -> Ok("1.1.0").  [generalization triangulation]
    Covers: future-version parse.

--- Group B: version floor (AC: MIN_VERSION=(1,0,0); reject <1.0.0 with guidance; accept 1.0.0/1.1.0; stale 0.5.0 text removed) ---
T5  min_version_const_is_1_0_0
    assert kina_cli::core::apple_container::MIN_VERSION == (1,0,0).  [degenerate constant check]
    Covers: "MIN_VERSION is (1,0,0)".
T6  validate_version_rejects_0_12_3
    validate_version("0.12.3").is_err().  [happy reject path]
    Covers: "rejects 0.12.3".
T7  validate_version_error_names_1_0_0_and_migration
    err string from validate_version("0.12.3") contains "1.0.0" AND mentions config.toml AND caps AND output-shape (assert on lowercased substrings: "config.toml", "1.0.0", and one of {"cap","capab"}, and {"shape" or "output"}).
    Covers: "error naming 1.0.0 and upgrade guidance (config.toml replaces property get/set/clear; structured ls/inspect shape changed; caps reduced)".
T8  validate_version_error_omits_stale_0_5_0_text
    err string from validate_version("0.12.3") does NOT contain "container images" NOR "0.5.0" NOR "com.apple.container.registry".
    Covers: "stale 0.5.0 breaking-changes text removed".
T9  validate_version_accepts_1_0_0
    validate_version("1.0.0").is_ok().  [boundary - exact floor accepted]
    Covers: "accepts 1.0.0".
T10 validate_version_accepts_1_1_0
    validate_version("1.1.0").is_ok().
    Covers: "accepts 1.1.0".

--- Group C: CLI path ordering (AC: PATH resolution before hardcoded fallback) ---
T11 cli_path_candidates_path_resolution_first
    let c = cli_path_candidates(); assert first element is CliPathStrategy::Which(_) (PATH/which strategy), and that at least one Hardcoded(_) exists AFTER the last Which index.
    Covers: "detect_cli_path prefers PATH (which container) BEFORE hardcoded fallback paths".
T12 cli_path_candidates_which_targets_container
    the first Which(name) has name == "container" (the brew/PATH binary), proving `which container` runs before any /usr/local/bin/container hardcoded entry.
    Covers: "stale /usr/local/bin/container no longer shadows brew 1.0.0 binary".

--- Group D: container-list JSON parsing degenerate/happy (AC: pub parse fn consuming 1.0.0 shape; live fixture verbatim) ---
